title: Senior Product Manager - RPay
description: Teamwork makes the stream work. Roku is changing how the world watches TVRoku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.  About the Team & Role The Roku Pay, Core Payments team has played a pivotal role in driving Roku’s rapid growth in online subscriptions, powered by a seamless in-streaming, on-TV purchase experience. Supporting a wide range of Roku teams, Core Payments enables transactions across multiple consumer touchpoints — including TV, web, mobile, and advertising. These teams employ diverse strategies to sell digital content, from Subscription Video on Demand (SVOD) and Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) to Roku-branded devices and shoppable ads directly on the device.Core Payments is now seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the creation of our Payment Machine Learning Platform — a high-impact initiative designed to optimize payment retries, routing, and authorization success rates for our subscription business. This platform will harness AI/ML to make real-time, data-driven payment decisions at scale, directly influencing revenue growth, reducing churn, and enhancing customer satisfaction. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of machine learning, payments infrastructure, and subscription growth, collaborating with engineering, data science, finance, and payment operations to deliver a platform that is both technically sophisticated and mission-critical. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, highly technical environment, possesses deep knowledge of payments and subscription economics, and can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. You will own the roadmap, drive flawless delivery, and continuously improve the platform through experimentation and data-driven insights. What You’ll Be Doing Define and Drive the Roadmap: Partner with engineering, ML, and business stakeholders to define a high-impact roadmap for the Payment ML Platform, aligned to Roku’s subscription growth goals.Translate Business Goals into Product Requirements/Feature Guides, Convert business KPIs (e.g., authorization rate uplift, churn reduction) into clear product requirements, success metrics, and technical specifications. Lead AI/ML Product Development: Collaborate with ML engineers and data scientists to design, train, and deploy models for retry prediction, payment routing, and fraud prevention.Champion Experimentation:  Design and oversee A/B and multivariate tests to validate hypotheses, measure incremental lift, and drive continuous improvement.Hands-On Execution: Roll up your sleeves to write PRDs, define acceptance criteria, review model performance dashboards, and troubleshoot issues with engineering.Stakeholder Management: Communicate roadmap progress, risks, and learnings to executives and cross-functional partners.Post-Launch Optimization: Monitor KPIs, identify opportunities for improvement, and prioritize enhancements to maximize impact.Ensure Compliance & Security: Work with legal and compliance teams to meet PCI-DSS, GDPR, and other regulatory requirements for payment data.  We’re Excited If You Have 8+ years in product management, ideally with experience in payments, fintech, subscription businesses, or ML-driven platforms. Proven track record of shipping complex products in partnership with engineering, data science, and operations teams. Strong understanding of payment processing flows, gateways, acquirer optimization, and retry logic. Experience defining and measuring KPIs, running large-scale A/B tests, and making data-driven decisions. Ability to articulate complex ML concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfort working in fast-paced, highly iterative environments. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field; MBA a plus.    Our Hybrid Work ApproachRoku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy. BenefitsRoku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter. AccommodationsRoku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com. The Roku CultureRoku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.
descriptionHtml:
Teamwork makes the stream work.

 

Roku is changing how the world watches TV

Roku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.

From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.

 

About the Team & Role 

The Roku Pay, Core Payments team has played a pivotal role in driving Roku’s rapid growth in online subscriptions, powered by a seamless in-streaming, on-TV purchase experience. Supporting a wide range of Roku teams, Core Payments enables transactions across multiple consumer touchpoints — including TV, web, mobile, and advertising. These teams employ diverse strategies to sell digital content, from Subscription Video on Demand (SVOD) and Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) to Roku-branded devices and shoppable ads directly on the device.

Core Payments is now seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the creation of our Payment Machine Learning Platform — a high-impact initiative designed to optimize payment retries, routing, and authorization success rates for our subscription business.
This platform will harness AI/ML to make real-time, data-driven payment decisions at scale, directly influencing revenue growth, reducing churn, and enhancing customer satisfaction. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of machine learning, payments infrastructure, and subscription growth, collaborating with engineering, data science, finance, and payment operations to deliver a platform that is both technically sophisticated and mission-critical.
The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, highly technical environment, possesses deep knowledge of payments and subscription economics, and can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. You will own the roadmap, drive flawless delivery, and continuously improve the platform through experimentation and data-driven insights. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

  • Define and Drive the Roadmap: Partner with engineering, ML, and business stakeholders to define a high-impact roadmap for the Payment ML Platform, aligned to Roku’s subscription growth goals.
  • Translate Business Goals into Product Requirements/Feature Guides, Convert business KPIs (e.g., authorization rate uplift, churn reduction) into clear product requirements, success metrics, and technical specifications. 
  • Lead AI/ML Product Development: Collaborate with ML engineers and data scientists to design, train, and deploy models for retry prediction, payment routing, and fraud prevention.
  • Champion Experimentation:  Design and oversee A/B and multivariate tests to validate hypotheses, measure incremental lift, and drive continuous improvement.
  • Hands-On Execution: Roll up your sleeves to write PRDs, define acceptance criteria, review model performance dashboards, and troubleshoot issues with engineering.
  • Stakeholder Management: Communicate roadmap progress, risks, and learnings to executives and cross-functional partners.
  • Post-Launch Optimization: Monitor KPIs, identify opportunities for improvement, and prioritize enhancements to maximize impact.
  • Ensure Compliance & Security: Work with legal and compliance teams to meet PCI-DSS, GDPR, and other regulatory requirements for payment data. 

 

We’re Excited If You Have 

  • 8+ years in product management, ideally with experience in payments, fintech, subscription businesses, or ML-driven platforms. 
  • Proven track record of shipping complex products in partnership with engineering, data science, and operations teams. 
  • Strong understanding of payment processing flows, gateways, acquirer optimization, and retry logic. 
  • Experience defining and measuring KPIs, running large-scale A/B tests, and making data-driven decisions. 
  • Ability to articulate complex ML concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. 
  • Comfort working in fast-paced, highly iterative environments. 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field; MBA a plus. 
 

 

Our Hybrid Work Approach

Roku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy.

 

Benefits

Roku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter.

 

Accommodations

Roku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com.

 

The Roku Culture

Roku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. 

We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. 

To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.

By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.

title: Sr Product Manager - Marketplace Item setup
description: About us:As a Fortune 50 company with more than 400,000 team members worldwide, Target is an iconic brand and one of America's leading retailers. Joining Target means promoting a culture of mutual care and respect and striving to make the most meaningful and positive impact. Becoming a Target team member means joining a community that values different voices and lifts each other up. Here, we believe your unique perspective isimportant, and you'll build relationships by being authentic and respectful.Team overview:Right along with Target’s distinctive retail brand, we are constantly improving and innovating our digital shopping experience. Are you ready to excel in the fast-changing retail environment? Are you a customer service enthusiast who’s curious about the guest and can see the shopping journey through their eyes? Then you’ll have success on the Digital Strategy & Platform team, whose goal is to offer guests a seamless online shopping experience across our platforms—including Target.com and the store network’s fulfillment capabilities. Here, you’ll collaborate to create and implement the digital strategies that enrich the online experience through the right mix of personalization, simplicity and intuitiveness. Your ideas will support Target in complementing the beloved in-store shopping interaction with a convenient, reliable and instantly gratifying digital experience.Role overview:We are seeking a Sr Product Manager - T+ (Marketplace) Item Charter to define and execute the strategy for how Target+ engages, guides, and enables sellers at scale. You will lead the strategy, vision, and execution of our Seller Portal. You will shape the ecosystem that empowers sellers, expands guest choice, and delivers scalable growthIn this role, you will lead a charter that improves seller activation, compliance, performance, and adoption across the marketplace lifecycle. You will own the systems, signals, and experiences that shape seller behavior—driving measurable improvements in marketplace quality, operational efficiency, and seller success.This is a highly cross-functional, data-driven role requiring strong product judgment, systems thinking, and the ability to influence outcomes without authority. You will partner closely with engineering, design, operations, and analytics teams to deliver intelligent, scalable solutions that help sellers take the right actions at the right time.As a Sr Product Manager, you will take lead on - Owning seller lifecycle engagement, including activation, adoption, performance improvement, and re-engagement. Designing data-driven nudges, reminders, and interventions that influence seller behavior and improve compliance, quality, and productivity. Enabling sellers to easily understand what actions they need to take and why, reducing friction and support dependency. Driving measurable behavior change in seller success. Define KPIs for marketplace success Use data and experimentation (A/B testing, cohort analysis) to refine features and improve adoption. Drive AI/ML powered insightsAbout you: Four-year degree or equivalent experience.6+ years of hands-on Product Management experience or equivalent domain experienceProven success in building and scaling marketplaces particularly in building seller-facing platforms - Item setup / catalog / PIM systems; Seller onboarding and listing workflowsOperational tools, workflows, and error management systemsStrong collaboration across business, tech, and operations teamsExceptional leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.Ability to think strategically while driving operational excellence.Must-Have Experience:Core experience in marketplaces (3P sellers, merchant tools)Ownership of catalog/item setup or listing systemsExperience building or improving seller onboarding / ingestion pipelinesStrong background in workflow systems, ops tooling, and error handlingProven cross-functional leadership (business + engineering + ops)End-to-end product ownership (beyond feature-level scope)Useful Links:Life at Target- https://india.target.com/Benefits- https://india.target.com/life-at-target/workplace/benefitsCulture- https://india.target.com/life-at-target/diversity-and-inclusion
descriptionHtml:
About us:

As a Fortune 50 company with more than 400,000 team members worldwide, Target is an iconic brand and one of America's leading retailers. Joining Target means promoting a culture of mutual care and respect and striving to make the most meaningful and positive impact. Becoming a Target team member means joining a community that values different voices and lifts each other up. Here, we believe your unique perspective is

important, and you'll build relationships by being authentic and respectful.


Team overview:

Right along with Target’s distinctive retail brand, we are constantly improving and innovating our digital shopping experience. Are you ready to excel in the fast-changing retail environment? Are you a customer service enthusiast who’s curious about the guest and can see the shopping journey through their eyes? Then you’ll have success on the Digital Strategy & Platform team, whose goal is to offer guests a seamless online shopping experience across our platforms—including Target.com and the store network’s fulfillment capabilities. Here, you’ll collaborate to create and implement the digital strategies that enrich the online experience through the right mix of personalization, simplicity and intuitiveness. Your ideas will support Target in complementing the beloved in-store shopping interaction with a convenient, reliable and instantly gratifying digital experience.


Role overview:

We are seeking a Sr Product Manager - T+ (Marketplace) Item Charter to define and execute the strategy for how Target+ engages, guides, and enables sellers at scale. You will lead the strategy, vision, and execution of our Seller Portal. You will shape the ecosystem that empowers sellers, expands guest choice, and delivers scalable growth

In this role, you will lead a charter that improves seller activation, compliance, performance, and adoption across the marketplace lifecycle. You will own the systems, signals, and experiences that shape seller behavior—driving measurable improvements in marketplace quality, operational efficiency, and seller success.


This is a highly cross-functional, data-driven role requiring strong product judgment, systems thinking, and the ability to influence outcomes without authority. You will partner closely with engineering, design, operations, and analytics teams to deliver intelligent, scalable solutions that help sellers take the right actions at the right time.


As a Sr Product Manager, you will take lead on - Owning seller lifecycle engagement, including activation, adoption, performance improvement, and re-engagement. Designing data-driven nudges, reminders, and interventions that influence seller behavior and improve compliance, quality, and productivity. Enabling sellers to easily understand what actions they need to take and why, reducing friction and support dependency. Driving measurable behavior change in seller success. Define KPIs for marketplace success Use data and experimentation (A/B testing, cohort analysis) to refine features and improve adoption. Drive AI/ML powered insights


About you:

  • Four-year degree or equivalent experience.
  • 6+ years of hands-on Product Management experience or equivalent domain experience
  • Proven success in building and scaling marketplaces particularly in building seller-facing platforms - Item setup / catalog / PIM systems; Seller onboarding and listing workflows
  • Operational tools, workflows, and error management systems
  • Strong collaboration across business, tech, and operations teams
  • Exceptional leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to think strategically while driving operational excellence.


Must-Have Experience:

  • Core experience in marketplaces (3P sellers, merchant tools)
  • Ownership of catalog/item setup or listing systems
  • Experience building or improving seller onboarding / ingestion pipelines
  • Strong background in workflow systems, ops tooling, and error handling
  • Proven cross-functional leadership (business + engineering + ops)
  • End-to-end product ownership (beyond feature-level scope)


Useful Links:

  • Life at Target- https://india.target.com/
  • Benefits- https://india.target.com/life-at-target/workplace/benefits
  • Culture- https://india.target.com/life-at-target/diversity-and-inclusion

descriptionHtml:
As a Senior Product Manager specializing in Payments, you will drive the vision, strategy, and execution of innovative payment solutions that enable seamless, secure, and scalable transactions for our customers. You will collaborate with cross-functional teamsincluding engineering, marketing, sales, finance, legal and go-to-marketto deliver global products that delight users, ensure regulatory compliance, and support business growth. This is a highly visible role requiring global payments expertise, technical fluency, and a passion for customer-centric innovation and growth.


In this role, you will:

  • Own and articulate the product vision and roadmap for Cvent payment products, ensuring alignment with company goals, evolving market trends, and regulatory requirements.
  • Identify opportunities to expand our payment capabilities across the Cvent ecosystem through new products and/or partnerships
  • Lead the end-to-end product lifecycle: ideation, problem definition, opportunity sizing, prioritization, development, launch, and ongoing iteration.
  • Represent the voice of the customer by gathering feedback and translating customer needs and insights into actionable product opportunities.
  • Define and analyse key metrics to measure customer and product success, leveraging data-driven decision making to inform strategy and prioritisation.
  • Collaborate closely with tech and non-tech stakeholders to deliver high-quality, global payment solutions on time.
  • Stay up-to-date on industry and technology trends, regulatory changes, and emerging payments technologies to inform product direction.
  • Leverage AI to identify and solve customer problems, enhance product capabilities and improve decision-making processes.
  • Identify key opportunities to use AI to externalise event payment capabilities and support AI-initiated workflows.
  • Evangelise the payments products internally and externally, representing Cvent at customer meetings, industry events, and online communities.


Required Qualifications

  • Bachelors degree or equivalent practical experience; advanced degree (MBA, MS) is a plus.
  • 12-16 years of total experience with at least 8-10+ years of product management experience, with a proven track record of delivering successful payment products at scale.
  • Deep understanding of the payments ecosystem, including roles involved, payment processors, global payment methods, networks, risk/fraud management, and regulatory compliance.
  • Experience leading cross-functional teams in a fast-paced, agile environment.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills; comfortable working with large data sets and analytics tools.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including experience presenting to executive leadership.
  • Technical background or experience working closely with engineering teams is highly desirable.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities and deliver results in a matrixed environment.
  • Passion for building products that solve real customer problems and drive measurable business impact.


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in SaaS, cloud, or large-scale B2B software environments.
  • Familiarity with AI and emerging technologies in the payments space.
  • Must have experience with payments platforms, global payments, digital wallets, and/or e-commerce platforms.

title: FBS Sr. Customer Experience Product Manager
description: The CAS (Customer Agent Solutions) Digital Service Operations team manages the E2E digital service operations for all digital channels. The team collaborates with multiple cross functional teams and bridges the gap between strategic goals and daily execution by day-to-day management of performance monitoring, defect management, optimization testing, infra and capabilities functions to ensure operations delivers on its daily/weekly/monthly handling objectives. Works as liaison between product and technology operations to reach the forecasted KPI targets for digital service customer experiences.This role operates at the intersection of digital operations, customer experience, and AI‑powered transformation. You will help drive the integration of intelligent automation, predictive analytics, and GenAI capabilities into digital service channels to improve customer experiences, enhance agent efficiency, and optimize operational performance.As a Customer Experience Digital Operations Manager, you will: Oversee end-to-end digital service operations across customer and agent journeys, partnering with Product, Technology, and Architecture teams to ensure smooth delivery of features, fixes, and enhancements Define and monitor KPIs such as experience scores, CSAT, FCR, and system uptime to track digital performance across various digital channels Drive real-time customer and agent experience tracking, identify friction points, and lead UX improvement initiatives backed by data analysis and user feedback Manage critical defects and high-priority escalations by coordinating with stakeholder teams to ensure rapid and accurate resolution Build and maintain real-time dashboards to surface trends, generate insights, and inform leadership decisions; leverage tools such as Qualtrics, Mixpanel, and Fullstory etc Use customer and partner research to strengthen analytical capabilities, identify market trends, and recommend customer experience enhancements Partner with data science, analytics, and engineering teams to embed AI-driven insights into digital operations, enabling proactive issue detection and resolution Apply AI-based predictive models to forecast demand, detect patterns, and inform operational and resource planning Evaluate and operationalize AI copilots, GenAI assistants, and automation tools to streamline agent workflows and improve customer self-service Promote responsible AI through governance, transparency, bias monitoring, explainability, and data privacy practices Lead GenAI-powered experiments that include personalized content, conversational experiences, and journey-level optimizations Support AI-driven journey analytics using NLP, clustering, sentiment analysis, and behavioral modeling to improve customer understandingKey Responsibilities Identify and scope opportunities to utilize AI, machine learning, and automation to optimize digital experience operations and agent support processes Collaborate with product owners to build AI‑informed business cases, including ROI modeling for predictive experiences, intelligent routing, and automated troubleshooting Manage AI‑enabled dashboards and monitoring systems that surface real‑time anomalies, experience dips, and emerging performance trends Partner with CX, analytics, and engineering teams to run A/B tests leveraging AI‑generated variants, measuring uplift and customer impact Translate customer and agent insights using AI‑powered analytics (e.g., text/sentiment analysis, pattern detection) into actionable recommendations for journey improvements Apply GenAI capabilities to improve team operations, e.g., automated insights summaries, triaging, root cause analysis drafting, and workflow documentationExperience & Education Requirements: Overall experience >8 years, 5+ years' experience required with extensive relevant customer understanding/user research/consulting experience, packaging and delivering insights to influence change. 6+ years of combined experience required in analytics, performance reporting, and/or process improvement Advanced project and process management experience. Agile preferred Bachelor's degree preferred in Technology, Mathematics, Statistics, Business, or related field Master's degree preferred in Management, Analytics, Artificial Intelligence Experience working with AI/ML‑driven platforms or analytics tools (e.g., customer behavioral modeling, NLP pipelines, predictive analytics) Background or coursework in AI, machine learning, data science, or human‑centered AI preferred Demonstrated ability to translate technical AI concepts into business language for non‑technical stakeholders Availability to work in PST time zoneRequirementsOther Critical Skills Familiarity with GenAI tools, conversational AI platforms, and AI‑assisted productivity solutions Ability to interpret outputs from models using techniques such as anomaly detection, clustering, and predictive scoring Strong understanding of AI ethics, governance, and responsible AI frameworks Comfortable working in environments where AI‑driven decision automation informs prioritization and operational strategies Possesses strong technical aptitude. Intermediate knowledge in using analytic tools such as Tableau and Microsoft Excel. Proficient in Microsoft Office including MS Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint Strong verbal communication and listening skills. Strong business acumen, with effective written and verbal communication skills Ability to communicate, influence, and deliver with cross-functional teams and enterprise stakeholders. Ability to manage multiple projects with tight deadlines effectively with cross functional teamsSoftware / Tool Skills Experience with AI/ML or data intelligence platforms (e.g., Azure ML, AWS SageMaker, Google Vertex AI—basic exposure acceptable) Hands‑on familiarity with GenAI copilots, prompt engineering concepts, or LLM‑based experience analysis tools Experience interpreting insights from NLP, AI‑powered text analytics, or conversational AI systems Hands on experience with user Feedback/Data analysis tools - Qualtrics, Fullstory, Mixpanel etc Good proficiency or practical experience of A/B Testing tools Agile tools such as Jira, Rally etc Understanding of Contact center and CMS tools Excel - Intermediate/Advanced. Proficiency in Microsoft 365 Power BI (Highly Preferred)BenefitsCompetitive compensation and benefits package:Competitive salary and performance-based bonusesComprehensive benefits packageCareer development and training opportunitiesFlexible work arrangements (remote and/or office-based)Dynamic and inclusive work culture within a globally renowned groupPrivate Health InsurancePension PlanPaid Time OffTraining & DevelopmentNote: Benefits differ based on employee level.About CapgeminiCapgemini is a global leader in partnering with companies to transform and manage their business by harnessing the power of technology. The Group is guided everyday by its purpose of unleashing human energy through technology for an inclusive and sustainable future. It is a responsible and diverse organization of over 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong 55-year heritage and deep industry expertise, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to address the entire breadth of their business needs, from strategy and design to operations, fueled by the fast evolving and innovative world of cloud, data, AI, connectivity, software, digital engineering and platforms. The Group €22.5 billion in revenues in 2023.https://www.capgemini.com/us-en/about-us/who-we-are/
descriptionHtml:
The CAS (Customer Agent Solutions) Digital Service Operations team manages the E2E digital service operations for all digital channels. The team collaborates with multiple cross functional teams and bridges the gap between strategic goals and daily execution by day-to-day management of performance monitoring, defect management, optimization testing, infra and capabilities functions to ensure operations delivers on its daily/weekly/monthly handling objectives. Works as liaison between product and technology operations to reach the forecasted KPI targets for digital service customer experiences.

This role operates at the intersection of digital operations, customer experience, and AI‑powered transformation. You will help drive the integration of intelligent automation, predictive analytics, and GenAI capabilities into digital service channels to improve customer experiences, enhance agent efficiency, and optimize operational performance.

As a Customer Experience Digital Operations Manager, you will:

  • Oversee end-to-end digital service operations across customer and agent journeys, partnering with Product, Technology, and Architecture teams to ensure smooth delivery of features, fixes, and enhancements
  • Define and monitor KPIs such as experience scores, CSAT, FCR, and system uptime to track digital performance across various digital channels
  • Drive real-time customer and agent experience tracking, identify friction points, and lead UX improvement initiatives backed by data analysis and user feedback
  • Manage critical defects and high-priority escalations by coordinating with stakeholder teams to ensure rapid and accurate resolution
  • Build and maintain real-time dashboards to surface trends, generate insights, and inform leadership decisions; leverage tools such as Qualtrics, Mixpanel, and Fullstory etc
  • Use customer and partner research to strengthen analytical capabilities, identify market trends, and recommend customer experience enhancements
  • Partner with data science, analytics, and engineering teams to embed AI-driven insights into digital operations, enabling proactive issue detection and resolution
  • Apply AI-based predictive models to forecast demand, detect patterns, and inform operational and resource planning
  • Evaluate and operationalize AI copilots, GenAI assistants, and automation tools to streamline agent workflows and improve customer self-service
  • Promote responsible AI through governance, transparency, bias monitoring, explainability, and data privacy practices
  • Lead GenAI-powered experiments that include personalized content, conversational experiences, and journey-level optimizations
  • Support AI-driven journey analytics using NLP, clustering, sentiment analysis, and behavioral modeling to improve customer understanding

Key Responsibilities

  • Identify and scope opportunities to utilize AI, machine learning, and automation to optimize digital experience operations and agent support processes
  • Collaborate with product owners to build AI‑informed business cases, including ROI modeling for predictive experiences, intelligent routing, and automated troubleshooting
  • Manage AI‑enabled dashboards and monitoring systems that surface real‑time anomalies, experience dips, and emerging performance trends
  • Partner with CX, analytics, and engineering teams to run A/B tests leveraging AI‑generated variants, measuring uplift and customer impact
  • Translate customer and agent insights using AI‑powered analytics (e.g., text/sentiment analysis, pattern detection) into actionable recommendations for journey improvements
  • Apply GenAI capabilities to improve team operations, e.g., automated insights summaries, triaging, root cause analysis drafting, and workflow documentation

Experience & Education Requirements:

  • Overall experience >8 years, 5+ years' experience required with extensive relevant customer understanding/user research/consulting experience, packaging and delivering insights to influence change. 6+ years of combined experience required in analytics, performance reporting, and/or process improvement
  • Advanced project and process management experience. Agile preferred
  • Bachelor's degree preferred in Technology, Mathematics, Statistics, Business, or related field
  • Master's degree preferred in Management, Analytics, Artificial Intelligence
  • Experience working with AI/ML‑driven platforms or analytics tools (e.g., customer behavioral modeling, NLP pipelines, predictive analytics)
  • Background or coursework in AI, machine learning, data science, or human‑centered AI preferred
  • Demonstrated ability to translate technical AI concepts into business language for non‑technical stakeholders
  • Availability to work in PST time zone

Requirements

Other Critical Skills

  • Familiarity with GenAI tools, conversational AI platforms, and AI‑assisted productivity solutions
  • Ability to interpret outputs from models using techniques such as anomaly detection, clustering, and predictive scoring
  • Strong understanding of AI ethics, governance, and responsible AI frameworks
  • Comfortable working in environments where AI‑driven decision automation informs prioritization and operational strategies
  • Possesses strong technical aptitude. Intermediate knowledge in using analytic tools such as Tableau and Microsoft Excel. Proficient in Microsoft Office including MS Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint
  • Strong verbal communication and listening skills. Strong business acumen, with effective written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to communicate, influence, and deliver with cross-functional teams and enterprise stakeholders. Ability to manage multiple projects with tight deadlines effectively with cross functional teams

Software / Tool Skills

  • Experience with AI/ML or data intelligence platforms (e.g., Azure ML, AWS SageMaker, Google Vertex AI—basic exposure acceptable)
  • Hands‑on familiarity with GenAI copilots, prompt engineering concepts, or LLM‑based experience analysis tools
  • Experience interpreting insights from NLP, AI‑powered text analytics, or conversational AI systems
  • Hands on experience with user Feedback/Data analysis tools - Qualtrics, Fullstory, Mixpanel etc
  • Good proficiency or practical experience of A/B Testing tools
  • Agile tools such as Jira, Rally etc
  • Understanding of Contact center and CMS tools
  • Excel - Intermediate/Advanced. Proficiency in Microsoft 365
  • Power BI (Highly Preferred)

Benefits

Competitive compensation and benefits package:

  • Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
  • Comprehensive benefits package
  • Career development and training opportunities
  • Flexible work arrangements (remote and/or office-based)
  • Dynamic and inclusive work culture within a globally renowned group
  • Private Health Insurance
  • Pension Plan
  • Paid Time Off
  • Training & Development

Note: Benefits differ based on employee level.

About Capgemini

Capgemini is a global leader in partnering with companies to transform and manage their business by harnessing the power of technology. The Group is guided everyday by its purpose of unleashing human energy through technology for an inclusive and sustainable future. It is a responsible and diverse organization of over 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong 55-year heritage and deep industry expertise, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to address the entire breadth of their business needs, from strategy and design to operations, fueled by the fast evolving and innovative world of cloud, data, AI, connectivity, software, digital engineering and platforms. The Group €22.5 billion in revenues in 2023.

https://www.capgemini.com/us-en/about-us/who-we-are/

descriptionHtml:
Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators.

 

Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely!

Overview:

Join our Data Integration team and take ownership of the Connect CDC product line. You will translate market trends and customer insights into a clear product roadmap. You will work closely with engineering, design, and go-to-market teams to deliver high-quality solutions. As the subject matter expert on Connect CDC, you will guide product development and identify growth opportunities. You will ensure the product continues to meet the evolving demands of our 12,000 customers worldwide. This is an individual contributor role with significant impact and visibility.

What you will do:

  • Define and own the Connect CDC roadmap, prioritizing capabilities that address customer needs and drive competitive differentiation in data integration and Change Data Capture.
  • Conduct ongoing research with customers, prospects, and analysts to surface unmet needs and inform product direction.
  • Author clear business requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria; maintain a well-prioritized backlog in close collaboration with engineering.
  • Partner with Engineering, UX, Support, and GTM teams throughout the product lifecycle to ensure on-time, high-quality delivery.
  • Monitor the CDC and data integration landscape and develop a working understanding of technical concepts such as connectors, pipelines, replication, and cloud/hybrid deployment.
  • Align product direction with internal stakeholders across Product, Sales, and Customer Success; serve as the go-to resource on Connect CDC capabilities and strategy.

What we are looking for:

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. A background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field is helpful but not required.
  • Experience: 5–7 years of product management experience building SaaS platforms, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.
  • Demonstrated ability to design and deliver reusable platform components—such as entitlement management, usage metering, tenant administration, and API-driven services—that support cross-team development at scale.
  • Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams.
  • Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha! or similar roadmapping and backlog management platforms.
  • Familiarity with data integration concepts such as CDC, ETL/ELT, connectors, or real-time data pipelines.
  • Travel is required: No.
  • Bonus points for: experience with hybrid cloud deployments (particularly AWS and MongoDB), hands-on familiarity with Kubernetes or API integration frameworks, exposure to CDC technologies, background in data quality or data governance, or familiarity with SQL, databases, or streaming platforms such as Kafka.

The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Senior Product Manager (Integrations)
description: Cvent is a leading meetings, events, and hospitality technology provider with more than 5,000+ employees and 24,000+ customers worldwide, including 60% of the Fortune 500. Founded in 1999, Cvent delivers a comprehensive event marketing and management platform for marketers and event professionals and offers software solutions to hotels, special event venues and destinations to help them grow their group/MICE and corporate travel business. Our technology brings millions of people together at events around the world. In short, we’re transforming the meetings and events industry through innovative technology that powers the human connection. Cvent's strength lies in its people, fostering a culture where everyone is encouraged to think like entrepreneurs, taking risks and making decisions confidently. We value diverse perspectives and celebrate differences, working together with colleagues and clients to build strong connections.AI at Cvent: Leading the FutureAre you ready to shape the future of work at the intersection of human expertise and AI innovation? descriptionHtml:
Cvent is a leading meetings, events, and hospitality technology provider with more than 5,000+ employees and 24,000+ customers worldwide, including 60% of the Fortune 500. Founded in 1999, Cvent delivers a comprehensive event marketing and management platform for marketers and event professionals and offers software solutions to hotels, special event venues and destinations to help them grow their group/MICE and corporate travel business. Our technology brings millions of people together at events around the world. In short, we’re transforming the meetings and events industry through innovative technology that powers the human connection.

Cvent's strength lies in its people, fostering a culture where everyone is encouraged to think like entrepreneurs, taking risks and making decisions confidently. We value diverse perspectives and celebrate differences, working together with colleagues and clients to build strong connections.


AI at Cvent: Leading the Future

Are you ready to shape the future of work at the intersection of human expertise and AI innovation? At Cvent, we’re committed to continuous learning and adaptation—AI isn’t just a tool for us, it’s part of our DNA. We’re looking for candidates who are eager to evolve alongside technology. If you love to experiment boldly, share your discoveries, and help define best practices for AI-augmented work, you’ll thrive here. Our team values professionals who thoughtfully integrate AI into their daily work, delivering exceptional results while relying on the human judgment and creativity that drive real innovation.


Throughout our interview process, you’ll have the chance to demonstrate how you use AI to learn, iterate, and amplify your impact. If you’re excited to be part of a team that’s leading the way in AI-powered collaboration, we’d love to meet you.


Disclaimer: Beware of Recruitment Scams – Legitimate Cvent recruiting communications will always come from an official ‘name@cvent.com’ email. We never request any payments or ask for sensitive personal or financial information via chat or social media platforms. For more information, please visit: https://www.cvent.com/en/notice-recruitment-fraud


Responsibilities

  • Develop an in-depth understanding of the business, products, including its goals and challenges, as well as a comprehensive knowledge of the customers and users of the platform. This involves analyzing their needs, behaviors, and pain points to ensure the product aligns with their expectations and delivers meaningful value.
  • Collaborate regularly with product managers on the future roadmap, including integration initiatives and the SFDC roadmap. Brainstorm new ideas and prioritize features to ensure the backlog remains healthy, well-organized, and aligned with business goals.
  • Become a subject matter expert in your product domain, possessing in-depth knowledge and insights that make you the primary point of contact for internal teams seeking guidance and collaboration.
  • Develop detailed product specifications that begin by clearly articulating the “why” - the core purpose and value behind the product, feature, or enhancements. These specifications should be thorough in addressing all necessary aspects yet written in a clear and concise manner to ensure they are easily understood by all stakeholders.
  • Write comprehensive user stories with detailed acceptance criteria that address all functional and non-functional aspects of the product, including security, data compliance, and other essential requirements.
  • Work closely with engineering, design (UX/UI), and other stakeholders to define product requirements and user stories.
  • Lead end-to-end product development process, from concept and design to development, testing, launch and ensuring timely and high-quality delivery.
  • Lead PI (Program Increment) planning and actively participate in key SCRUM ceremonies, including daily standups, backlog refinement, sprint planning, and product demos.
  • Should possess a solid grasp of technology platforms, integrations, and system design to collaborate effectively with engineering partners.
  • Leverage the power of analytics and data-driven intelligence to assess product performance, user behavior analysis, gather customer feedback, and insights from multiple channels, and make data-informed decisions which enables you to drive excellence through iteration, improvement, and refinement.
  • Monitor and analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to gauge the effectiveness, adoption, and product outcome.
  • Share and present regular updates on the product roadmap and progress to leaders and stakeholders. Clearly highlight any potential risks and outline a plan to address them, ensuring everyone is informed and aligned on the path forward.


Qualifications


  • Minimum 10-15 years of career experience with at least 5 years of product management (PO/PM) experience in technology products.
  • Strong product sense with an ability to articulate problems and envision solution
  • Strong understanding of technology and software development processes, with the ability to communicate effectively with technical teams.
  • Inquisitive mindset with excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
  • Experience defining, launching, and evolving APIs and integrations at scale; clear understanding of API fundamentals (e.g., REST/GraphQL, authentication/authorization, versioning, pagination, idempotency, error handling, and rate limiting).
  • Advocate and embed an AI-first mindset, integrating AI capabilities where they create strategic advantage, improve efficiency, or unlock new customer value.
  • Proven ability to work hands-on with engineering and architecture teams; comfortable reading API specs, discussing system design, and making tradeoffs across performance, scalability, and cost.
  • Ability to manage competing and evolving priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Ability to handle complex situations, conversations and navigate ambiguity.
  • Exceptional interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence, partner and build relationships across all levels of the organization.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills with an ability to communicate directly and clearly.
  • Demonstrated experience working on at least one integration project, from discovery through delivery and measurement.

Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators.

Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely!

Precisely is an AI-first organization. All employees are expected to demonstrate proficiency in applying AI tools to accelerate their work, improve output quality, and eliminate low-value tasks. Candidates should be comfortable using generative AI tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT) in their day-to-day workflows, able to evaluate AI-generated outputs critically, and open to continuously adopting new AI capabilities as they emerge.

 

Overview:

Join our Product Management team and become a driving force behind the Master Data Management Product (MDM) group. In this pivotal role, you'll delve into market trends across our existing and target customer segments, transforming insights into actionable business requirements, functional use cases, and compelling product solutions involving AI. Collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, you'll emerge as the subject matter expert on MDM, guiding product development and supporting sales with your deep product knowledge and market insights. Your expertise will identify growth opportunities, inform strategy, and ensure our products continue to meet the evolving needs of our market.

What you will do:

  • Manage Product Requirements: Gather and prioritize product needs from customers, sales, support, and other teams. Create clear documentation (MRDs and PRDs), and work with Engineering to build the roadmaps.
  • Develop Business Plans: Collaborate and contribute to create long-term business plans for selected market segments. Track performance and update stakeholders regularly.
  • Build Business Cases: Support product decisions, use of AI, and partnerships with solid business models and justifications.
  • Communicate Effectively: Share updates clearly through documents, emails, and presentations. Ensure stakeholders understand market trends and product direction.
  • Provide Leadership: Be the voice of the customer and influence the direction of the product.
  • Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Marketing, Engineering, Strategic Planning, and other product teams to make informed decisions and expand product influence.
  • Track Success Metrics: Monitor and report on key performance indicators to ensure goals are being met.

What we are looking for:

  • Experience: 8+ years of product management experience building data products, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. Background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or related fields is helpful but not required.
  • Product Management Expertise: Demonstrated ability to design and deliver solutions specifically in product information management or other data domains—such as material master, customer master, reference data, etc.
  • Experience with a broad spectrum of data management practices, including data quality, data integration, data governance, and metadata management.
  • Experience with AI: Demonstrated expertise in justifying and embedding AI in products.
  • Hybrid Cloud Expertise: Experience with hybrid cloud deployments and SaaS solutions, particularly in AWS.
  • Strategic & Execution Skills: Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams and a high-volume feature backlog.
  • Conceptual Technical Proficiency: Comfortable partnering with engineering on high-level architectural decisions involving APIs, integrations, platform services, and hybrid deployment models.
  • Tools & Methodology Experience: Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha!. Demonstrated ability to influence without authority and lead cross-functional initiatives across product lines.
  • Enthusiasm to work in a fast-paced, demanding business environment while collaborating effectively with cross-functional groups, management, sales and customers.
  • Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage high volumes of work
  • Excellent analytical, interpersonal, managerial, and organizational skills

The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Senior Product Manager
description: Senior Product Manager – Platform Efficiency & COGSAbout The RoleAs a Senior Product Manager for Platform Efficiency, you will drive continual “cost to value” alignment by delivering the product features and platform enhancements that ensure durable efficiency of scale. descriptionHtml:
Senior Product Manager – Platform Efficiency & COGS

About The Role

As a Senior Product Manager for Platform Efficiency, you will drive continual “cost to value” alignment by delivering the product features and platform enhancements that ensure durable efficiency of scale. You will sit at the intersection of Product, Engineering, FinOps, and Pricing - responsible for the architectural efficiency and gross margin health of our global SaaS analytics platform.

Sumo Logic processes hundreds of terabytes of data daily. Your mission is to ensure that as we scale, our infrastructure costs stay lean and our platform remains a performant, competitive, and high-margin engine. You will own the strategy for optimizing data ingestion, storage, networking, and compute costs while simultaneously improving the platform’s performance and reliability.

Key Responsibilities

  • COGS Strategy & Roadmap: Own the end-to-end roadmap for platform efficiency. Identify, prioritize, and execute initiatives that reduce cost-to-serve without compromising customer experience or SLAs. Own product strategy to focus on customer margins across multiple Sumologic revenue products
  • Infrastructure Optimization: Collaborate with Core Platform and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) teams to optimize resource allocation across Kubernetes, Kafka, and AWS services (S3, DynamoDB, EC2).
  • Cost Observability: Partner with FinOps to ensure internal "Cost Observability" dashboards inform continual efficiency improvements. Use data to identify "hot spots" in our AWS/Cloud infrastructure and drive targeted architectural shifts.
  • Product Improvement Lifecycle: Translate complex technical bottlenecks into actionable PRDs. Define "Definition of Done" for efficiency projects, focusing on metrics like Cost per GB Ingested and Cost of Query engine, CPU utilisation of engineering assemblies.
  • Cross-Functional Leadership: Coordinate across Finance, UX, and Engineering stakeholders to deliver cost optimized solutions that meet the needs of customers as well as the financial goals of the business. Clearly articulate the financial impacts of technical debt and infrastructure modernization to executive stakeholders.
  • Vendor & Cloud Management: Work with cloud providers and 3rd party vendors to optimize licensing, reserved instances, and usage-based billing models.

What We’re Looking For

  • Technical Depth: You have a solid understanding of cloud-native architectures, distributed systems, and microservices. Experience with AWS cost structures and FinOps principles is a major plus.
  • Data-Driven Mindset: You are a power user of data analytics tools and have a strong hold on cost and numbers. You don’t just report data; you find the "why" behind the numbers to drive strategic pivots.
  • SaaS Savvy: 5+ years of PM experience, ideally in a technical platform role within an Enterprise SaaS environment (Log Analytics, Observability, or Cybersecurity).
  • Execution Focus: Proven track record of taking high-level efficiency goals and breaking them down into testable hypotheses and delivered features.
  • Communication: Ability to explain the ROI of a "refactoring project" to a non-technical stakeholder just as clearly as you can discuss API rate-limiting with a Principal Engineer.

Pricing Knowledge: Experience with pricing, units of measure, and cost modeling.

 Technical Toolkit (Nice to Have)

  • Experience with Big Data technologies (Lucene, Parquet, Spark).
  • Familiarity with billing and provisioning systems (e.g., Zuora).
  • Understanding of OpenTelemetry (OTel) and its impact on data pipeline efficiency.

Why Join Sumo Logic?

You will be working on one of the largest scale data problems in the industry. Your work directly impacts the company’s bottom line and determines our ability to innovate by freeing up capital for new product investments.

About Us

Sumo Logic, Inc. helps make the digital world secure, fast, and reliable by unifying critical security and operational data through its Intelligent Operations Platform. Built to address the increasing complexity of modern cybersecurity and cloud operations challenges, we empower digital teams to move from reaction to readiness—combining agentic AI-powered SIEM and log analytics into a single platform to detect, investigate, and resolve modern challenges. Customers around the world rely on Sumo Logic for trusted insights to protect against security threats, ensure reliability, and gain powerful insights into their digital environments. For more information, visit www.sumologic.com.

Sumo Logic Privacy Policy. Employees will be responsible for complying with applicable federal privacy laws and regulations, as well as organizational policies related to data protection.

descriptionHtml:
The CAS (Customer Agent Solutions) Digital Service Operations team manages the E2E digital service operations for all digital channels. The team collaborates with multiple cross functional teams and bridges the gap between strategic goals and daily execution by day-to-day management of performance monitoring, defect management, optimization testing, infra and capabilities functions to ensure operations delivers on its daily/weekly/monthly handling objectives. Works as liaison between product and technology operations to reach the forecasted KPI targets for digital service customer experiences.

This role operates at the intersection of digital operations, customer experience, and AI‑powered transformation. You will help drive the integration of intelligent automation, predictive analytics, and GenAI capabilities into digital service channels to improve customer experiences, enhance agent efficiency, and optimize operational performance.

As a Customer Experience Digital Operations Manager, you will:

  • Oversee end-to-end digital service operations across customer and agent journeys, partnering with Product, Technology, and Architecture teams to ensure smooth delivery of features, fixes, and enhancements
  • Define and monitor KPIs such as experience scores, CSAT, FCR, and system uptime to track digital performance across various digital channels
  • Drive real-time customer and agent experience tracking, identify friction points, and lead UX improvement initiatives backed by data analysis and user feedback
  • Manage critical defects and high-priority escalations by coordinating with stakeholder teams to ensure rapid and accurate resolution
  • Build and maintain real-time dashboards to surface trends, generate insights, and inform leadership decisions; leverage tools such as Qualtrics, Mixpanel, and Fullstory etc
  • Use customer and partner research to strengthen analytical capabilities, identify market trends, and recommend customer experience enhancements
  • Partner with data science, analytics, and engineering teams to embed AI-driven insights into digital operations, enabling proactive issue detection and resolution
  • Apply AI-based predictive models to forecast demand, detect patterns, and inform operational and resource planning
  • Evaluate and operationalize AI copilots, GenAI assistants, and automation tools to streamline agent workflows and improve customer self-service
  • Promote responsible AI through governance, transparency, bias monitoring, explainability, and data privacy practices
  • Lead GenAI-powered experiments that include personalized content, conversational experiences, and journey-level optimizations
  • Support AI-driven journey analytics using NLP, clustering, sentiment analysis, and behavioral modeling to improve customer understanding

Key Responsibilities

  • Identify and scope opportunities to utilize AI, machine learning, and automation to optimize digital experience operations and agent support processes
  • Collaborate with product owners to build AI‑informed business cases, including ROI modeling for predictive experiences, intelligent routing, and automated troubleshooting
  • Manage AI‑enabled dashboards and monitoring systems that surface real‑time anomalies, experience dips, and emerging performance trends
  • Partner with CX, analytics, and engineering teams to run A/B tests leveraging AI‑generated variants, measuring uplift and customer impact
  • Translate customer and agent insights using AI‑powered analytics (e.g., text/sentiment analysis, pattern detection) into actionable recommendations for journey improvements
  • Apply GenAI capabilities to improve team operations, e.g., automated insights summaries, triaging, root cause analysis drafting, and workflow documentation

Experience & Education Requirements:

  • Overall experience >8 years, 5+ years' experience required with extensive relevant customer understanding/user research/consulting experience, packaging and delivering insights to influence change. 6+ years of combined experience required in analytics, performance reporting, and/or process improvement
  • Advanced project and process management experience. Agile preferred
  • Bachelor's degree preferred in Technology, Mathematics, Statistics, Business, or related field
  • Master's degree preferred in Management, Analytics, Artificial Intelligence
  • Experience working with AI/ML‑driven platforms or analytics tools (e.g., customer behavioral modeling, NLP pipelines, predictive analytics)
  • Background or coursework in AI, machine learning, data science, or human‑centered AI preferred
  • Demonstrated ability to translate technical AI concepts into business language for non‑technical stakeholders
  • Availability to work in PST time zone

Requirements

Other Critical Skills

  • Familiarity with GenAI tools, conversational AI platforms, and AI‑assisted productivity solutions
  • Ability to interpret outputs from models using techniques such as anomaly detection, clustering, and predictive scoring
  • Strong understanding of AI ethics, governance, and responsible AI frameworks
  • Comfortable working in environments where AI‑driven decision automation informs prioritization and operational strategies
  • Possesses strong technical aptitude. Intermediate knowledge in using analytic tools such as Tableau and Microsoft Excel. Proficient in Microsoft Office including MS Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint
  • Strong verbal communication and listening skills. Strong business acumen, with effective written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to communicate, influence, and deliver with cross-functional teams and enterprise stakeholders. Ability to manage multiple projects with tight deadlines effectively with cross functional teams

Software / Tool Skills

  • Experience with AI/ML or data intelligence platforms (e.g., Azure ML, AWS SageMaker, Google Vertex AI—basic exposure acceptable)
  • Hands‑on familiarity with GenAI copilots, prompt engineering concepts, or LLM‑based experience analysis tools
  • Experience interpreting insights from NLP, AI‑powered text analytics, or conversational AI systems
  • Hands on experience with user Feedback/Data analysis tools - Qualtrics, Fullstory, Mixpanel etc
  • Good proficiency or practical experience of A/B Testing tools
  • Agile tools such as Jira, Rally etc
  • Understanding of Contact center and CMS tools
  • Excel - Intermediate/Advanced. Proficiency in Microsoft 365
  • Power BI (Highly Preferred)

Benefits

Competitive compensation and benefits package:

  • Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
  • Comprehensive benefits package
  • Career development and training opportunities
  • Flexible work arrangements (remote and/or office-based)
  • Dynamic and inclusive work culture within a globally renowned group
  • Private Health Insurance
  • Pension Plan
  • Paid Time Off
  • Training & Development

Note: Benefits differ based on employee level.

About Capgemini

Capgemini is a global leader in partnering with companies to transform and manage their business by harnessing the power of technology. The Group is guided everyday by its purpose of unleashing human energy through technology for an inclusive and sustainable future. It is a responsible and diverse organization of over 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong 55-year heritage and deep industry expertise, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to address the entire breadth of their business needs, from strategy and design to operations, fueled by the fast evolving and innovative world of cloud, data, AI, connectivity, software, digital engineering and platforms. The Group €22.5 billion in revenues in 2023.

https://www.capgemini.com/us-en/about-us/who-we-are/

descriptionHtml:
About Arcserve


Arcserve provides exceptional solutions to protect the priceless digital assets of organizations in need of full scale, comprehensive data protection. Established in 1983, Arcserve is the world’s most experienced provider of business continuity solutions that safeguard every application and system, on every premises and every cloud. Organizations in over 150 countries around the world rely on Arcserve’s highly efficient, integrated technologies and expertise to eliminate the risk of data loss and extended downtime while reducing the cost and complexity of backing up and restoring data by up to 50 percent


Position Overview

Arcserve is seeking an experienced Senior Product Manager to lead the strategy and delivery of data protection and cyber resilience solutions designed for the Managed Service Provider (MSP) market.

This role requires a strong blend of domain expertise in backup, disaster recovery, and cyber resilience, deep understanding of MSP business models, and hands-on experience leveraging AI, automation, and emerging agentic systems to drive innovation.

This is a remote role open to candidates in India.


Key Responsibilities

  • Define and execute product strategy and roadmap for MSP-focused data protection solutions
  • Design capabilities that support multi-tenancy, automation, scalability, and efficient MSP operations
  • Drive innovation in backup, ransomware protection, anomaly detection, and disaster recovery orchestration
  • Identify and deliver AI/automation opportunities, including predictive insights, workflow automation, and agent-driven operations
  • Partner with engineering and data teams to develop intelligent, automation-first features
  • Leverage AI tools to accelerate product workflows (research, documentation, analysis, prototyping)
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams and engage directly with customers and MSP partners
  • Own end-to-end product lifecycle, including metrics, prioritization, and Agile execution


Required Qualifications

  • 7+ years of product management experience in enterprise or B2B SaaS
  • Strong expertise in data protection, backup & recovery, DR, or cyber resilience
  • Proven experience building or managing products for the MSP market
  • Experience using AI tools to enhance PM productivity and workflows
  • Experience defining or contributing to AI/ML, automation, or agent-driven features
  • Solid understanding of MSP operations (multi-tenancy, service delivery, SLAs)
  • Experience with hybrid/cloud environments (AWS, Azure, on-prem)
  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with DRaaS/BaaS or SaaS data protection platforms
  • Familiarity with ransomware detection and cyber recovery workflows
  • Background in infrastructure (virtualization, storage, networking)
  • Experience with subscription or usage-based pricing models
