descriptionHtml:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Marketing at Revolut is all about clarity, creativity, and commercial impact. Our Growth team turns bold ideas into campaigns that connect with millions. We work across channels and teams to build a brand that earns attention and drives results, shaping how people see, understand, and engage with our products.

We’re looking for a world-class Product Marketing Manager that’ll articulate and drive marketing strategies to promote the positive impact of Revolut People and build trust within the industry. You’ll be working with our Revolut People team to bring innovative, seamless people management solutions to businesses.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Developing and executing content strategies to increase brand visibility and generate high-quality leads
  • Managing and creating organic social media content to enhance Revolut People’s presence and engagement
  • Owning the editorial calendar for executive thought leadership, helping position Revolut People’s leadership team as industry experts
  • Creating and optimising content assets, including whitepapers, case studies, landing pages, and gated materials
  • Repurposing website content into new SEO-driven pages to improve search visibility and attract inbound traffic
  • Collaborating with the Creative team to develop engaging visual assets for content distribution
  • Defining and executing content distribution strategies across organic channels to maximise impact

What You'll Need

  • 5+ years of experience in content marketing, preferably in tech or B2B SaaS
  • Expertise in organic demand generation, including SEO, content strategy, and social media
  • Experience working with creative teams to develop impactful marketing assets
  • Impressive copywriting and storytelling skills, with a portfolio of engaging content
  • A data-driven mindset and to be comfortable analysing performance metrics and optimising content strategies
  • The ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment
  • Fluency in English

Nice to have

  • Experience in fintech or a fast-scaling startup
  • Familiarity with marketing automation tools (HubSpot, Marketo, or similar)
  • Experience with other inbound-generating channels, such as social media, etc.

Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.

  • Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
  • Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.

We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Sr Product Manager, Shipping/Logistics
description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.eBay is a global commerce leader that allows you to shape how the world buys, sells, and gives. You’ll be part of a work culture that’s been genuinely committed to diversity and inclusion since its founding more than 20 years ago. Here, you can just be yourself, do your best work, and have a meaningful impact on people across the globe. We are looking for people with drive, ideas, and a passion for helping small businesses succeed to help shape the future of eBay—does this sound like you? If so, we’d love to talk to you!About The TeamWe are passionate about connecting buyers and sellers around the world and creating opportunities through commerce and technology. Our responsibilities include all aspects of logistics, to empower sellers through state-of-the-art shipping tools.About The RoleeBay is looking for an experienced Product Manager to execute and shape our shipping tools product strategy. Developing a platform to support and delight users from many distinct markets carries with it many challenges. Ideally you will have previously developed global product strategies that enable and drive innovation and that have launched with meaningful results. You will work closely with our regional business leaders, product and domain leads to identify and build core capabilities, improve utility of features common to multiple key partners, and also solve for local conditions critical to market success.Key ResponsibilitiesDefine and execute product strategy and roadmap for Shipping tools backed up by user needs and data; have a global viewpoint and extend applications across use casesAs the Product Owner, provide clear product vision to your scrum team and co-lead the Agile scrum team with engineering counterpart to execute on roadmap with clear milestonesClearly set the product/backlog prioritisation with a balance of near-term deliverables and longer-term investment in technology platformsBe the product evangelist and partner closely with business and engineering leadersEffectively communicate strategies & trade-offs up to senior leaders and down to development teamJob RequirementsHands-on experience with agile development processExpertise and/or passion in technologies and products required to bring shipping and logistics solutions to market, at scale and globallyDesign, collect, analyse and benchmark product data to make decisions and business casesManage and optimise business processes to free up developers; do everything you can to speed up work that adds value to users directlyAsks the right questions to formulate product perspective and present requirementsBalances technical and business acumen; able to communicate what is possible and what will have the biggest impact on businessBuilds positive relationships internally and externally; an active listener and clear communicatorBasic Qualification7+ years of strong product management experienceExperience in shipping/logistics domain an advantageKnowledge of SQL and Tableau an advantagePast experience in product development or platform product management would be idealBenefitsBenefits are an essential part of your total compensation for the work you do every day. Whether you’re single, in a growing family, or nearing retirement, eBay offers a variety of comprehensive and competitive benefit programs to meet your needs. Including maternal & paternal leave, paid sabbatical, and plans to help ensure your financial security today and in the years ahead because we know feeling financially secure during your working years and through retirement is important.We love creating opportunities for others by connecting people from widely diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and geographies. So, being diverse and inclusive isn’t just something we strive for, it is who we are, and part of what we do each and every single day. We want to ensure that as an employee, you feel eBay is a place where, no matter who you are, you feel safe, included, and that you have the opportunity to bring your unique self to work.. To learn about our Diversity & Inclusion click here: https://www.ebayinc.com/our-company/diversity-inclusion/.Please see the Talent Privacy Notice for information regarding how eBay handles your personal data collected when you use the eBay Careers website or apply for a job with eBay.eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.The eBay Jobs website uses cookies to enhance your experience. By continuing to browse the site, you agree to our use of cookies. Visit our Privacy Center for more information.
descriptionHtml:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.

Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.

Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.

eBay is a global commerce leader that allows you to shape how the world buys, sells, and gives. You’ll be part of a work culture that’s been genuinely committed to diversity and inclusion since its founding more than 20 years ago. Here, you can just be yourself, do your best work, and have a meaningful impact on people across the globe. We are looking for people with drive, ideas, and a passion for helping small businesses succeed to help shape the future of eBay—does this sound like you? If so, we’d love to talk to you!

About The Team

We are passionate about connecting buyers and sellers around the world and creating opportunities through commerce and technology. Our responsibilities include all aspects of logistics, to empower sellers through state-of-the-art shipping tools.

About The Role

eBay is looking for an experienced Product Manager to execute and shape our shipping tools product strategy. Developing a platform to support and delight users from many distinct markets carries with it many challenges. Ideally you will have previously developed global product strategies that enable and drive innovation and that have launched with meaningful results. You will work closely with our regional business leaders, product and domain leads to identify and build core capabilities, improve utility of features common to multiple key partners, and also solve for local conditions critical to market success.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define and execute product strategy and roadmap for Shipping tools backed up by user needs and data; have a global viewpoint and extend applications across use cases
  • As the Product Owner, provide clear product vision to your scrum team and co-lead the Agile scrum team with engineering counterpart to execute on roadmap with clear milestones
  • Clearly set the product/backlog prioritisation with a balance of near-term deliverables and longer-term investment in technology platforms
  • Be the product evangelist and partner closely with business and engineering leaders
  • Effectively communicate strategies & trade-offs up to senior leaders and down to development team


Job Requirements

  • Hands-on experience with agile development process
  • Expertise and/or passion in technologies and products required to bring shipping and logistics solutions to market, at scale and globally
  • Design, collect, analyse and benchmark product data to make decisions and business cases
  • Manage and optimise business processes to free up developers; do everything you can to speed up work that adds value to users directly
  • Asks the right questions to formulate product perspective and present requirements
  • Balances technical and business acumen; able to communicate what is possible and what will have the biggest impact on business
  • Builds positive relationships internally and externally; an active listener and clear communicator


Basic Qualification

  • 7+ years of strong product management experience
  • Experience in shipping/logistics domain an advantage
  • Knowledge of SQL and Tableau an advantage
  • Past experience in product development or platform product management would be ideal


Benefits

Benefits are an essential part of your total compensation for the work you do every day. Whether you’re single, in a growing family, or nearing retirement, eBay offers a variety of comprehensive and competitive benefit programs to meet your needs. Including maternal & paternal leave, paid sabbatical, and plans to help ensure your financial security today and in the years ahead because we know feeling financially secure during your working years and through retirement is important.

We love creating opportunities for others by connecting people from widely diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and geographies. So, being diverse and inclusive isn’t just something we strive for, it is who we are, and part of what we do each and every single day. We want to ensure that as an employee, you feel eBay is a place where, no matter who you are, you feel safe, included, and that you have the opportunity to bring your unique self to work.. To learn about our Diversity & Inclusion click here: https://www.ebayinc.com/our-company/diversity-inclusion/.

Please see the Talent Privacy Notice for information regarding how eBay handles your personal data collected when you use the eBay Careers website or apply for a job with eBay.

eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.

The eBay Jobs website uses cookies to enhance your experience. By continuing to browse the site, you agree to our use of cookies. Visit our Privacy Center for more information.

title: Senior Product Manager
description: About Team:US Omni Tech at Walmart Global Tech, Bengaluru, is focused on improving Walmart customer experience across both physical & digital and making Walmart the most exciting place to shop. With 4000+ brick and mortar stores and thousands of other suppliers fulfilling Walmart's online/in-store orders for over 1 billion customers - the challenge and complexity is pronounced! We work within the world’s largest retailer yet maintain a startup feel working in small agile teams to revolutionize the fulfilment landscape. As a leader in the supply chain group, you are accountable and responsible for number of critical products/platforms that help serve our customers in the most cost-effective manner in multiple geographies and businesses.What you'll do:Assist in defining product features and translating business needs into user stories and technical requirements.Partner with engineering and QA teams to plan sprints and ensure timely, high-quality delivery.Analyze data and feedback to prioritize feature enhancements and resolve issues.Work with internal stakeholders (e.g., logistics, inventory, store operations) to gather and clarify requirements.Support in developing product documentation, training materials, and internal communication plans.Drive product capabilities and roadmap with very minimal supervision and guidanceBe technically savvy to build and grow technical platforms and products as well as contribute to engineering conversations & priorities in a scrappy, ambiguous start-up environmentBe an advocate of metric/data-based decision making, Make impact on a global scale.Promotes and supports mission, values, and standards of ethics and integrity.What you'll bring:You come with 3+ years of product management experience.Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a cross-functional teamExperience with eCommerce or internet-based products is a plus.Excellent communication and stakeholder management.Uncompromising commitment to the customer.Excellent written and verbal communication skills with ability to champion product solutions throughout the organization.Advanced/ master’s degree in engineering or management is preferred.About Walmart Global TechImagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered.We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.Flexible, hybrid workWalmart’s culture sets us apart, and we know being together helps us innovate, learn and grow great careers. This role is based in our [Bangalore/Chennai] office for daily work, with the flexibility for associates to manage their personal lives.BenefitsBeyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best-in-class benefits maternity and parental leave, PTO, health benefits, and much more.BelongingWe aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.At Walmart, our vision is "everyone included." By fostering a workplace culture where everyone is—and feels—included, everyone wins. Our associates and customers reflect the makeup of all 19 countries where we operate. By making Walmart a welcoming place where all people feel like they belong, we’re able to engage associates, strengthen our business, improve our ability to serve customers, and support the communities where we operate.Equal Opportunity EmployerWalmart, Inc., is an Equal Opportunities Employer – By Choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions – while being inclusive of all people.Minimum Qualifications...Outlined below are the required minimum qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no minimum qualifications.Option 1: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or related area and 5 years’ experience in product management. Option 2: 7 years’ experience in product management or related area.Preferred Qualifications...Outlined below are the optional preferred qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no preferred qualifications.Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or related area and 4 years’ experience as a product manager, ideally working on consumer-facing, large-scale, highly complex B2B/C productsMasters: Business AdministrationPrimary Location...Block- 1, Prestige Tech Pacific Park, Sy No. 38/1, Outer Ring Rd Kadubeesanahalli , India
descriptionHtml:
About Team:

US Omni Tech at Walmart Global Tech, Bengaluru, is focused on improving Walmart customer experience across both physical & digital and making Walmart the most exciting place to shop. With 4000+ brick and mortar stores and thousands of other suppliers fulfilling Walmart's online/in-store orders for over 1 billion customers - the challenge and complexity is pronounced! We work within the world’s largest retailer yet maintain a startup feel working in small agile teams to revolutionize the fulfilment landscape. As a leader in the supply chain group, you are accountable and responsible for number of critical products/platforms that help serve our customers in the most cost-effective manner in multiple geographies and businesses.

What you'll do:

Assist in defining product features and translating business needs into user stories and technical requirements.

Partner with engineering and QA teams to plan sprints and ensure timely, high-quality delivery.

Analyze data and feedback to prioritize feature enhancements and resolve issues.

Work with internal stakeholders (e.g., logistics, inventory, store operations) to gather and clarify requirements.

Support in developing product documentation, training materials, and internal communication plans.

Drive product capabilities and roadmap with very minimal supervision and guidance

Be technically savvy to build and grow technical platforms and products as well as contribute to engineering conversations & priorities in a scrappy, ambiguous start-up environment

Be an advocate of metric/data-based decision making, Make impact on a global scale.

Promotes and supports mission, values, and standards of ethics and integrity.

What you'll bring:

You come with 3+ years of product management experience.

Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a cross-functional team

Experience with eCommerce or internet-based products is a plus.

Excellent communication and stakeholder management.

Uncompromising commitment to the customer.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills with ability to champion product solutions throughout the organization.

Advanced/ master’s degree in engineering or management is preferred.

About Walmart Global Tech

Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered.

We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.

Flexible, hybrid work

Walmart’s culture sets us apart, and we know being together helps us innovate, learn and grow great careers. This role is based in our [Bangalore/Chennai] office for daily work, with the flexibility for associates to manage their personal lives.

Benefits

Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best-in-class benefits maternity and parental leave, PTO, health benefits, and much more.

Belonging

We aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.

At Walmart, our vision is "everyone included." By fostering a workplace culture where everyone is—and feels—included, everyone wins. Our associates and customers reflect the makeup of all 19 countries where we operate. By making Walmart a welcoming place where all people feel like they belong, we’re able to engage associates, strengthen our business, improve our ability to serve customers, and support the communities where we operate.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Walmart, Inc., is an Equal Opportunities Employer – By Choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions – while being inclusive of all people.

Minimum Qualifications...Outlined below are the required minimum qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no minimum qualifications.

Option 1: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or related area and 5 years’ experience in product management. Option 2: 7 years’ experience in product management or related area.

Preferred Qualifications...Outlined below are the optional preferred qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no preferred qualifications.

Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or related area and 4 years’ experience as a product manager, ideally working on consumer-facing, large-scale, highly complex B2B/C products

Masters: Business Administration

Primary Location...Block- 1, Prestige Tech Pacific Park, Sy No. 38/1, Outer Ring Rd Kadubeesanahalli , India

title: Senior Product Manager
sector: Data Security Software Products, Computer and Network Security, and Software Development
description: Congratulations, you have taken the first step towards bagging a career-defining role. Join the team of superheroes that safeguard data wherever it goes.What should you know about us? Seclore protects and controls digital assets to help enterprises prevent data theft and achieve compliance. Permissions and access to digital assets can be granularly assigned and revoked, or dynamically set at the enterprise-level, including when shared with external parties. Asset discovery and automated policy enforcement allow enterprises to adapt to changing security threats and regulatory requirements in real-time and at scale.Know more about us at www.seclore.comYou would love our tribe: If you are a risk-taker, innovator, and fearless problem solver who loves solving challenges of data security, thenthis is the place for you!A sneak peek into the role: We are fast growing product company and bringing next generation products in ‘secure your data’ domain. We are one of the few product companies in globe that can protect and control data wherever it travels. This is the opportunity to build an exciting and high impact product. Security is core to what we build, and we’re looking for a Product Manager who’s passionate about building secure, scalable, and user-centric products.Seclore seeks an experienced Product Manager with deep expertise in managing large-scale data, its architecture, and transforming it into actionable insights, along with strong technical expertise and client-facing skills.This role involves launching a new product, connecting clients, industry experts, and our technical team to define data-driven use cases and outcomes. Strong communication skills, solid understanding of data platforms and analytics, and efficient project management in complex, data-rich environments are essential.Here's what you will get to explore: Requirements Gathering & DefinitionCollaborate with security architects, engineers, and compliance teams to gather technical, data, and regulatory requirements, including how large-scale data is captured, stored, and protected.Translate complex cybersecurity and data architecture needs into clear product specifications, data contracts, and user stories that guide implementation.Understand end-user journeys and instrument product experiences to gather usage, performance, and security metrics, enabling evaluation of new features’ effectiveness and data-driven decision-making.Be the Functional ExpertUnderstand the full Seclore product suite functionally and be the expert for the product modules owned by you, including how they integrate with enterprise data platforms and pipelines.Know various user personas, their business context, and their data needs, such as reporting, analytics, and insight generation.Understand the technology environment, including devices, operating systems, browsers, and the underlying data architecture (data flows, storage, APIs, and integrations) that powers product capabilities.Product Development SupportPartner with engineering teams throughout the development lifecycle to ensure secure design and implementation of data flows and storage patterns at scale.Participate in sprint planning, backlog grooming, and daily stand-ups to provide product and data-related guidance, clarify instrumentation requirements, and prioritize insight-generating features.Security IntegrationEnsure security is embedded in all stages of the product lifecycle while balancing observability, logging, and analytics needs for large volumes of product data.Work on integrating security tools and frameworks (e.g., SIEM, IAM, DLP,into the product ecosystem and associated data pipelines, ensuring events are captured and modeled for actionable insights.Technical DocumentationCreate and maintain detailed documentation for security features, APIs, workflows, data models, and compliance mappings, including how product data is collected and used for analytics.Cross-Functional CollaborationAct as a liaison between product, engineering, security, data, and compliance teams to align on data strategy, metrics, and architecture decisions.Support Product leaders and GTM leaders with technical and data insights, feasibility assessments, and recommendations on how to leverage Seclore data for customer outcomes.Risk & Compliance AlignmentEnsure product features and data handling practices align with internal security policies and external descriptionHtml:
Congratulations, you have taken the first step towards bagging a career-defining role. Join the team of superheroes that safeguard data wherever it goes.



What should you know about us?


Seclore protects and controls digital assets to help enterprises prevent data theft and achieve compliance. Permissions and access to digital assets can be granularly assigned and revoked, or dynamically set at the enterprise-level, including when shared with external parties. Asset discovery and automated policy enforcement allow enterprises to adapt to changing security threats and regulatory requirements in real-time and at scale.


Know more about us at www.seclore.com



You would love our tribe:


If you are a risk-taker, innovator, and fearless problem solver who loves solving challenges of data security, then

this is the place for you!



A sneak peek into the role:


We are fast growing product company and bringing next generation products in ‘secure your data’ domain. We are one of the few product companies in globe that can protect and control data wherever it travels. This is the opportunity to build an exciting and high impact product. Security is core to what we build, and we’re looking for a Product Manager who’s passionate about building secure, scalable, and user-centric products.


Seclore seeks an experienced Product Manager with deep expertise in managing large-scale data, its architecture, and transforming it into actionable insights, along with strong technical expertise and client-facing skills.


This role involves launching a new product, connecting clients, industry experts, and our technical team to define data-driven use cases and outcomes. Strong communication skills, solid understanding of data platforms and analytics, and efficient project management in complex, data-rich environments are essential.



Here's what you will get to explore:


Requirements Gathering & Definition

  • Collaborate with security architects, engineers, and compliance teams to gather technical, data, and regulatory requirements, including how large-scale data is captured, stored, and protected.
  • Translate complex cybersecurity and data architecture needs into clear product specifications, data contracts, and user stories that guide implementation.
  • Understand end-user journeys and instrument product experiences to gather usage, performance, and security metrics, enabling evaluation of new features’ effectiveness and data-driven decision-making.


Be the Functional Expert

  • Understand the full Seclore product suite functionally and be the expert for the product modules owned by you, including how they integrate with enterprise data platforms and pipelines.
  • Know various user personas, their business context, and their data needs, such as reporting, analytics, and insight generation.
  • Understand the technology environment, including devices, operating systems, browsers, and the underlying data architecture (data flows, storage, APIs, and integrations) that powers product capabilities.


Product Development Support

  • Partner with engineering teams throughout the development lifecycle to ensure secure design and implementation of data flows and storage patterns at scale.
  • Participate in sprint planning, backlog grooming, and daily stand-ups to provide product and data-related guidance, clarify instrumentation requirements, and prioritize insight-generating features.


Security Integration

  • Ensure security is embedded in all stages of the product lifecycle while balancing observability, logging, and analytics needs for large volumes of product data.
  • Work on integrating security tools and frameworks (e.g., SIEM, IAM, DLP,into the product ecosystem and associated data pipelines, ensuring events are captured and modeled for actionable insights.


Technical Documentation

  • Create and maintain detailed documentation for security features, APIs, workflows, data models, and compliance mappings, including how product data is collected and used for analytics.


Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Act as a liaison between product, engineering, security, data, and compliance teams to align on data strategy, metrics, and architecture decisions.
  • Support Product leaders and GTM leaders with technical and data insights, feasibility assessments, and recommendations on how to leverage Seclore data for customer outcomes.


Risk & Compliance Alignment

  • Ensure product features and data handling practices align with internal security policies and external regulations (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS), including data retention and access controls.
  • Assist in security audits and risk assessments by providing clear visibility into product data flows, architecture, and evidence from product usage analytics.



We can see the next Entrepreneur at Seclore if you have:


  • A technical degree (Engineering, MCA) and business degree (MBA) from a reputed institute.
  • 5+ years of experience in B2B product management, technical program management, or cybersecurity engineering.
  • Experience working in Agile environments and collaborating closely with engineering teams.
  • Ability to break down complex technical problems into simple, actionable steps.
  • Strong analytical, communication, and organizational skills.
  • Experience working in a software product organization as a product consultant, product owner, business analyst or functional expert in the product team.
  • Proven experience working with large-scale data platforms, understanding data architecture, and using product data to derive actionable insights and drive decision-making.
  • Technical background in software either from software development or systems perspective.


GOOD TO HAVE:


  • Awareness and exposure to User Experience design practices.
  • Experience in information security procedures and practices.
  • Initiative to take up responsibility and ownership of tasks and ensure end to end delivery and completion with the highest level of quality.



Why do we call Seclorites Entrepreneurs not Employees?


  • We value and support those who take the initiative and calculate risks.
  • We have an attitude of a problem solver and an aptitude that is tech agnostic. You get to work with the smartest minds in the business.
  • We are thriving not living. At Seclore, it is not just about work but about creating outstanding employee experiences. Our supportive and open culture enables our team to thrive.



Excited to be the next Entrepreneur, apply today!


Don’t have some of the above points in your resume at the moment? Don’t worry. We will help you build it.


Let’s build the future of data security at Seclore together.

descriptionHtml:
Job Summary

Matterway is an AI Assistant helping operational teams in large enterprises by automating repetitive tasks and guiding decision making. Help us reinvent how teams work by putting cutting edge generative AI and automation tools into the hands of everyday business users. You will work across multiple products in the Matterway suite - a cloud platform for process optimization AI coding agents and specialized AI agents.

Responsibilities

We are looking for a Product Manager who loves talking to customers can translate fuzzy problems into crisp product bets and ships elegant software at a high velocity

What you will do

Senior Product Manager AI platform

Discover real problems

  • Run user interviews shadow user workflows and join sales and support calls
  • Synthesize pain points into actionable insights

Frame and shape opportunities

  • Frame problems by integrating user needs market dynamics and data driven signals and estimate the effort worth investing
  • Write lean PRDs or specs and sketch solutions in Figma or as quick prototypes
  • Coordinate with leadership and functional leads to evaluate prioritize and staff opportunities for execution

Steward the user experience

  • Champion intuitive functional and beautiful design across the product
  • Own UAT and partner with QA to uphold quality

Drive delivery

  • Maintain and groom the opportunity backlog
  • Coordinate with the engineering team to structure delivery plans align on scope and keep momentum by eliminating blockers swiftly
  • Keep a pulse on progress through transparent tracking and ensure the team stays on course toward high velocity delivery

Launch learn iterate

  • Orchestrate go live changelogs communications and enablement
  • Monitor post launch analytics and optimize for adoption

Level up how we work

  • Curate playbooks FAQs and internal wikis so new teammates hit the ground running
  • Continuously refine our tools and processes

Jump in where needed

  • Prompt engineer prototype test whatever moves the product forward. You are not afraid to get your hands dirty

What makes you a great fit

Must haves

  • Product Management track record You have led product cycles end to end in cross functional teams and shipped software in high velocity environments
  • Great product sense You know what good feels like and can explain the why.
  • Exceptional communication in English You craft crisp narratives and talk tech design and business with equal ease.
  • Interpersonal and Influence Skills skilled in facilitation stakeholder empathy expectation management and leading without authority
  • Technical fluency You can read code and understand technical constraints when making product decisions
  • AI native Strong understanding of AI technologies LLM principles and prompt engineering skills
  • A growth mindset Willingness to own problems. Comfort with ambiguity. A desire to make things better. A balanced sense of optimism and humility

Nice-to-haves

  • Proficient in Figma with a solid understanding of modern design systems
  • Industry experience in BFSI Procurement HR or in general with Enterprise internal workflows
  • Background in RPA BPM low-code or other automation domains
  • Dual degree or equivalent experience spanning Engineering and Business or Design.

title: Senior Product Manager/Product Manager - Platform
description: About WatiStarted as a WhatsApp team inbox in 2020, Wati has evolved into a full revenue orchestration system that goes beyond a single platform. We empower businesses that sell, support, and grow through conversations by observing customer intent in real-time, deciding the next best revenue action, and executing it seamlessly across marketing, sales, and support—all within WhatsApp and connected messaging channels.Our Platform & AI CapabilitiesWati is designed for scalability and intelligence. Our AI-native platform simplifies complex customer communication operations through a unified inbox, a robust multi-channel messaging infrastructure, and no-code automation. At the heart of our solution is Astra, our intelligent AI layer, which helps you create AI Agents for all customer interactions and all your messaging platforms. By integrating AI agents into the ecosystem, we enable businesses of all sizes to deliver measurable ROI and build deeper customer relationships.Our Backing & Partnerships Trusted by over 16,000 customers across 190+ countries, Wati is proudly backed by world-class investors including Tiger Global, Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and Shopify. As a Premium-tier Partner of Meta and Google, we maintain the highest standards of platform excellence and integration.About The RoleWati is growing rapidly and we are looking to hire a Senior Product Manager to help scale our product offerings and work with our product and engineering teams to develop and execute our shared product vision. We are looking for extremely entrepreneurial PMs who value moving quickly.If you have demonstrated experience building APIs & MCPs, understand developer communities, have empathy for both consumers and businesses and want to unlock the potential for messaging to serve millions of customers, we would be excited to hear from you.This will be an opportunity for you to build experiences that thousands of small businesses use on a daily basis, and to be a key part of this high growth journey.What you'll do:  Own the product strategy and roadmap for building native integrations, strong APIs and an Agent friendly platform Identify high-impact integration and ecosystem opportunities by analyzing developer needs, Agent behaviours, partner platforms, and competitive landscapes Work closely with engineering to define APIs, SDKs, auth, and extensibility primitives that scale and are easy to adopt Build a product-led partnerships strategy that turns integrations into a repeatable demand and revenue channel Lead 0→1 and early-scale experiments for new integrations, marketplaces and communities; measuring adoption, activation, and revenue impact Collaborate with sales, partnership and marketing teams to launch, position, and grow integrations and drive adoption amongst developer communities Synthesize learnings and present clear recommendations to leadershipRequirementsExperience as a Senior Product Manager at a scale or growth-stage company, ideally on developer, platform, or ecosystem productsStrong technical product judgment; comfortable working with AI platforms, APIs, MCPs, integrations, LLM tooling, and developer and agentic workflowsTrack record of launching new platforms and iterating based on real usage and dataAbility to operate with ambiguity while building systems that scaleData-driven mindset with clear communication and stakeholder management skillsNice to have:Experience at a startup or high-growth scaleupCoding or SQL experience — you don't need to ship code, but you can read it and get your own answers from dataFamiliarity with the messaging, CPaaS, or WhatsApp ecosystemBachelor's in Computer Science or a related technical field
descriptionHtml:
About Wati

Started as a WhatsApp team inbox in 2020, Wati has evolved into a full revenue orchestration system that goes beyond a single platform. We empower businesses that sell, support, and grow through conversations by observing customer intent in real-time, deciding the next best revenue action, and executing it seamlessly across marketing, sales, and support—all within WhatsApp and connected messaging channels.

Our Platform & AI Capabilities

Wati is designed for scalability and intelligence. Our AI-native platform simplifies complex customer communication operations through a unified inbox, a robust multi-channel messaging infrastructure, and no-code automation. At the heart of our solution is Astra, our intelligent AI layer, which helps you create AI Agents for all customer interactions and all your messaging platforms. By integrating AI agents into the ecosystem, we enable businesses of all sizes to deliver measurable ROI and build deeper customer relationships.

Our Backing & Partnerships

Trusted by over 16,000 customers across 190+ countries, Wati is proudly backed by world-class investors including Tiger Global, Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and Shopify. As a Premium-tier Partner of Meta and Google, we maintain the highest standards of platform excellence and integration.

About The Role

Wati is growing rapidly and we are looking to hire a Senior Product Manager to help scale our product offerings and work with our product and engineering teams to develop and execute our shared product vision. We are looking for extremely entrepreneurial PMs who value moving quickly.

If you have demonstrated experience building APIs & MCPs, understand developer communities, have empathy for both consumers and businesses and want to unlock the potential for messaging to serve millions of customers, we would be excited to hear from you.

This will be an opportunity for you to build experiences that thousands of small businesses use on a daily basis, and to be a key part of this high growth journey.

What you'll do:

  • Own the product strategy and roadmap for building native integrations, strong APIs and an Agent friendly platform
  • Identify high-impact integration and ecosystem opportunities by analyzing developer needs, Agent behaviours, partner platforms, and competitive landscapes
  • Work closely with engineering to define APIs, SDKs, auth, and extensibility primitives that scale and are easy to adopt
  • Build a product-led partnerships strategy that turns integrations into a repeatable demand and revenue channel
  • Lead 0→1 and early-scale experiments for new integrations, marketplaces and communities; measuring adoption, activation, and revenue impact
  • Collaborate with sales, partnership and marketing teams to launch, position, and grow integrations and drive adoption amongst developer communities
  • Synthesize learnings and present clear recommendations to leadership

Requirements

  • Experience as a Senior Product Manager at a scale or growth-stage company, ideally on developer, platform, or ecosystem products
  • Strong technical product judgment; comfortable working with AI platforms, APIs, MCPs, integrations, LLM tooling, and developer and agentic workflows
  • Track record of launching new platforms and iterating based on real usage and data
  • Ability to operate with ambiguity while building systems that scale
  • Data-driven mindset with clear communication and stakeholder management skills

Nice to have:

  • Experience at a startup or high-growth scaleup
  • Coding or SQL experience — you don't need to ship code, but you can read it and get your own answers from data
  • Familiarity with the messaging, CPaaS, or WhatsApp ecosystem
  • Bachelor's in Computer Science or a related technical field

descriptionHtml:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Product Manager, Scheduling Platform in India.

This role sits at the core of a highly strategic platform powering scheduling capabilities across multiple products and workflows. You will own and evolve the foundation that enables how users book, plan, and coordinate critical actions across the ecosystem. The work directly impacts product scalability, integration depth, and overall customer experience across several teams. You will operate in a high-autonomy environment where product managers are expected to lead discovery, execution, and iteration with strong alignment across engineering, design, and go-to-market teams. This is a platform-heavy role requiring both strategic thinking and hands-on execution. You will shape long-term architectural decisions while ensuring incremental delivery of value. The ideal environment for someone who enjoys solving complex system-level problems with real user impact.

Accountabilities

Role

In this role, you will define and evolve the scheduling platform that powers multiple product experiences and integrations across the organization:

  • Own the product strategy, roadmap, and execution for the Scheduling Platform, ensuring alignment with broader company goals
  • Lead discovery efforts to understand user workflows, identify platform gaps, and define scalable scheduling capabilities
  • Partner with internal product teams to consolidate needs and design reusable platform solutions instead of one-off features
  • Drive roadmap prioritization, sequencing, and delivery of platform investments balancing short-term value and long-term scalability
  • Collaborate closely with engineering teams on architecture, technical tradeoffs, and phased implementation planning
  • Manage dependencies across integrations, feature teams, and external systems to ensure consistent platform evolution
  • Communicate platform strategy, risks, and business impact clearly to leadership and cross-functional stakeholders
  • Use data, observability tools, and system insights to monitor platform health and inform product decisions

Requirements

You bring strong product leadership experience in complex, technical environments and are comfortable shaping foundational platform capabilities:

  • 5+ years of product management experience, ideally in platform, infrastructure-adjacent, or integration-heavy products
  • Proven ability to design scalable systems and reusable capabilities rather than isolated feature solutions
  • Strong execution skills with experience driving complex, cross-functional initiatives from discovery to delivery
  • Ability to work effectively with engineering teams on API design, system architecture, and technical tradeoffs
  • Comfortable operating in ambiguity and bringing clarity to complex product and technical spaces
  • Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to connect platform decisions to business outcomes
  • Experience collaborating with engineering, design, analytics, support, and go-to-market teams
  • Familiarity with APIs, integration patterns, system design concepts, and platform dependencies
  • Ability to work with data and observability tools (e.g., dashboards, monitoring systems) to assess platform behavior

Benefits

  • Remote-first work environment based in India with flexible working arrangements
  • Competitive compensation aligned with experience and impact
  • Opportunity to own a critical platform shaping core product experiences at scale
  • High-autonomy culture with strong ownership and decision-making responsibility
  • Collaborative, cross-functional environment with engineering and product peers
  • Career growth opportunities within a fast-evolving product organization
  • Exposure to complex platform architecture, integrations, and scalable system design challenges
  • Inclusive and supportive culture focused on learning and continuous improvement

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

descriptionHtml:
About Pocket Entertainment


Pocket Entertainment is a global leader in immersive audio storytelling, creating richly produced narrative experiences that engage millions of listeners across the world.

With over 100 billion minutes streamed annually, an average of 120 minutes of daily listening time, and more than 6 billion total audio plays, Pocket FM is on a mission to build the world’s largest AI-powered audio streaming platform, powered by a thriving community of 250,000+ creators. The platform continues to see rapid growth in the US and India, with strong expansion momentum across Europe and LATAM.


Our mission is to redefine the future of storytelling by empowering writers and creators worldwide with next-generation AI tools, from story generation co-pilots and seamless multilingual adaptations to expressive voice synthesis and creative tooling for image and video generation. As an AI-first company, we’re building and deploying proprietary generative AI models that power our entire value chain, spanning content creation, curation, marketing, distribution, and monetization.


What you’ll own?


  • Own and drive the strategy and roadmap for creators experience & retention on the platform for your charter
  • Partner cross-functionally with engineering, design, data, business & analytics to ship high-quality, impactful product features
  • Use data and user insights to inform prioritization and continuously iterate on product experiences
  • Effectively communicate complex ideas, fostering alignment among leadership and cross-functional teams
  • Collaborate with various product teams to develop solutions to shared challenges across multiple product lines
  • Ensure the pod delivers measurable impact on creator retention, user growth and revenue for the company


What we’re looking for?


  • 5+ years of product management experience, ideally in B2C. Previous experience in working on creator facing products is a plus
  • Proficient in analytical thinking and problem-solving, skilled at converting data insights into
  • Growth mindset with a passion for experimentation, A/B testing, user empathy and rapid iteration
  • Proficient in analytical thinking and problem-solving, skilled at converting data insights into practical strategies
  • Deep passion for designing and delivering exceptionally pleasing user experiences
  • Excellent cross-functional leadership: Engineering, Business, Design, and GTM teams
  • Deep passion for entertainment, storytelling, content creation, or the creator economy

title: Senior Product Manager
description: About Team:US Omni Tech at Walmart Global Tech, Bengaluru, is focused on improving Walmart customer experience across both physical & digital and making Walmart the most exciting place to shop. With 4000+ brick and mortar stores and thousands of other suppliers fulfilling Walmart's online/in-store orders for over 1 billion customers - the challenge and complexity is pronounced! We work within the world’s largest retailer yet maintain a startup feel working in small agile teams to revolutionize the fulfilment landscape. As a leader in the supply chain group, you are accountable and responsible for number of critical products/platforms that help serve our customers in the most cost-effective manner in multiple geographies and businesses.What you'll do:Assist in defining product features and translating business needs into user stories and technical requirements.Partner with engineering and QA teams to plan sprints and ensure timely, high-quality delivery.Analyze data and feedback to prioritize feature enhancements and resolve issues.Work with internal stakeholders (e.g., logistics, inventory, store operations) to gather and clarify requirements.Support in developing product documentation, training materials, and internal communication plans.Drive product capabilities and roadmap with very minimal supervision and guidanceBe technically savvy to build and grow technical platforms and products as well as contribute to engineering conversations & priorities in a scrappy, ambiguous start-up environmentBe an advocate of metric/data-based decision making, Make impact on a global scale.Promotes and supports mission, values, and standards of ethics and integrity.What you'll bring:You come with 5+ years of product management experience.Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a cross-functional teamExperience with eCommerce or internet-based products is a plus.Excellent communication and stakeholder management.Uncompromising commitment to the customer.Excellent written and verbal communication skills with ability to champion product solutions throughout the organization.Advanced/ master’s degree in engineering or management is preferred.About Walmart Global TechImagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. descriptionHtml:
About Team:

US Omni Tech at Walmart Global Tech, Bengaluru, is focused on improving Walmart customer experience across both physical & digital and making Walmart the most exciting place to shop. With 4000+ brick and mortar stores and thousands of other suppliers fulfilling Walmart's online/in-store orders for over 1 billion customers - the challenge and complexity is pronounced! We work within the world’s largest retailer yet maintain a startup feel working in small agile teams to revolutionize the fulfilment landscape. As a leader in the supply chain group, you are accountable and responsible for number of critical products/platforms that help serve our customers in the most cost-effective manner in multiple geographies and businesses.

What you'll do:

Assist in defining product features and translating business needs into user stories and technical requirements.

Partner with engineering and QA teams to plan sprints and ensure timely, high-quality delivery.

Analyze data and feedback to prioritize feature enhancements and resolve issues.

Work with internal stakeholders (e.g., logistics, inventory, store operations) to gather and clarify requirements.

Support in developing product documentation, training materials, and internal communication plans.

Drive product capabilities and roadmap with very minimal supervision and guidance

Be technically savvy to build and grow technical platforms and products as well as contribute to engineering conversations & priorities in a scrappy, ambiguous start-up environment

Be an advocate of metric/data-based decision making, Make impact on a global scale.

Promotes and supports mission, values, and standards of ethics and integrity.

What you'll bring:

You come with 5+ years of product management experience.

Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a cross-functional team

Experience with eCommerce or internet-based products is a plus.

Excellent communication and stakeholder management.

Uncompromising commitment to the customer.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills with ability to champion product solutions throughout the organization.

Advanced/ master’s degree in engineering or management is preferred.

About Walmart Global Tech

Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered.

We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.

Walmart’s culture sets us apart, and we know being together helps us innovate, learn and grow great careers. This role is based in our Bangalore office for daily work, with the flexibility for associates to manage their personal lives.

Benefits

Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best-in-class benefits maternity and parental leave, PTO, health benefits, and much more.

Belonging

We aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.

At Walmart, our vision is "everyone included." By fostering a workplace culture where everyone is—and feels—included, everyone wins. Our associates and customers reflect the makeup of all 19 countries where we operate. By making Walmart a welcoming place where all people feel like they belong, we’re able to engage associates, strengthen our business, improve our ability to serve customers, and support the communities where we operate.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Walmart, Inc., is an Equal Opportunities Employer – By Choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions – while being inclusive

Minimum Qualifications...Outlined below are the required minimum qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no minimum qualifications.

Option 1: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or related area and 5 years’ experience in product management. Option 2: 7 years’ experience in product management or related area.

Preferred Qualifications...Outlined below are the optional preferred qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no preferred qualifications.

Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or related area and 4 years’ experience as a product manager, ideally working on consumer-facing, large-scale, highly complex B2B/C products

Masters: Business Administration, Masters: Business Administration, Masters: Business Administration, Masters: Business Administration, Masters: Business Administration

Primary Location...Block- 1, Prestige Tech Pacific Park, Sy No. 38/1, Outer Ring Rd Kadubeesanahalli , India

title: Senior Product Manager - Subscriptions Scaling
description: Teamwork makes the stream work. Roku is changing how the world watches TVRoku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.  About the Team The Content Platform & Intelligence team builds the pipelines, tooling, and intelligence that power Roku’s global Sports, Live, and VOD experiences. We ingest and enrich partner catalogs at scale, ensure rights-accurate availability, and feed core platform experiences like Search, Browse, and Recommendations. We obsess over metadata quality, reliability, and performance so viewers can seamlessly discover and watch what they love.About the Role We’re seeking a Senior Product Manager to scale how Roku onboards and operates Premium Subscription partners. You will own the product strategy and execution for onboarding VOD, Live, and Sports content at scale; keeping content rights, volumes, and performance at the center. You’ll partner closely with Subscriptions, Metadata Platform, Sports & Live product teams, Content Operations, Partnerships, and Engineering to deliver high-quality integrations that power monetisation, discovery, and delightful viewer experiences.What You’ll DoOwn the roadmap for onboarding new Premium Subscription partners covering VOD, Live, and Sports. Collaborate with metadata providers to enhance Live & Sports coverage: schedules, fixtures, teams/players, scores, artwork, league hierarchies. Partner with the Content Platform team to ingest, normalise, and store content intelligence that drives Search and Recommendations. Model regional rights, windows, blackout rules, entitlements, and service-level policies to ensure the right content shows up for the right users. Build internal tools and workflows that minimise manual merchandising and ops, especially for new markets and complex sports/live schedules. We’re Excited If You Have7+ years of Product Management experience in platform/backend or content systems; streaming/media experience strongly preferred. Proven track record building scalable ingestion/onboarding platforms, catalog systems, or developer ecosystems. Experience with VOD/Live/Sports pipelines, content rights and availability modelling, and internationalisation. Experience partnering with ML/AI teams on metadata quality, enrichment, ranking, or recommendations; comfortable with experimentation at scale.    Our Hybrid Work ApproachRoku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy. BenefitsRoku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter. AccommodationsRoku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com. The Roku CultureRoku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.
descriptionHtml:
Teamwork makes the stream work.

 

Roku is changing how the world watches TV

Roku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.

From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.

 

About the Team 

The Content Platform & Intelligence team builds the pipelines, tooling, and intelligence that power Roku’s global Sports, Live, and VOD experiences. We ingest and enrich partner catalogs at scale, ensure rights-accurate availability, and feed core platform experiences like Search, Browse, and Recommendations. We obsess over metadata quality, reliability, and performance so viewers can seamlessly discover and watch what they love.

About the Role 

We’re seeking a Senior Product Manager to scale how Roku onboards and operates Premium Subscription partners. You will own the product strategy and execution for onboarding VOD, Live, and Sports content at scale; keeping content rights, volumes, and performance at the center. You’ll partner closely with Subscriptions, Metadata Platform, Sports & Live product teams, Content Operations, Partnerships, and Engineering to deliver high-quality integrations that power monetisation, discovery, and delightful viewer experiences.

What You’ll Do

  • Own the roadmap for onboarding new Premium Subscription partners covering VOD, Live, and Sports.
  • Collaborate with metadata providers to enhance Live & Sports coverage: schedules, fixtures, teams/players, scores, artwork, league hierarchies.
  • Partner with the Content Platform team to ingest, normalise, and store content intelligence that drives Search and Recommendations.
  • Model regional rights, windows, blackout rules, entitlements, and service-level policies to ensure the right content shows up for the right users.
  • Build internal tools and workflows that minimise manual merchandising and ops, especially for new markets and complex sports/live schedules.

We’re Excited If You Have

  • 7+ years of Product Management experience in platform/backend or content systems; streaming/media experience strongly preferred.
  • Proven track record building scalable ingestion/onboarding platforms, catalog systems, or developer ecosystems.
  • Experience with VOD/Live/Sports pipelines, content rights and availability modelling, and internationalisation.
  • Experience partnering with ML/AI teams on metadata quality, enrichment, ranking, or recommendations; comfortable with experimentation at scale.

 

 

Our Hybrid Work Approach

Roku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy.

 

Benefits

Roku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter.

 

Accommodations

Roku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com.

 

The Roku Culture

Roku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. 

We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. 

To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.

By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.
