descriptionHtml:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Product Manager, Scheduling Platform in India.

This role sits at the core of a mission-critical platform that powers scheduling across multiple products and customer workflows. You will define and evolve the strategy for a scalable scheduling foundation that enables teams across the organization to build, integrate, and innovate more effectively. Acting as a key driver of platform transformation, you will balance long-term architectural thinking with short-term delivery impact. You will collaborate closely with engineering, design, analytics, and cross-functional product teams to shape reusable capabilities that support a wide range of use cases. The role requires strong product leadership in a highly technical environment where scheduling is central to user experience and operational workflows. You will help unlock new product possibilities while ensuring reliability, flexibility, and performance at scale.

Accountabilities

In this role, you will own the strategy, roadmap, and execution of a foundational scheduling platform that supports multiple product teams and customer workflows.

  • Define and lead the product strategy and roadmap for the Scheduling Platform, ensuring scalability, flexibility, and long-term value.
  • Drive discovery for platform-level opportunities by analyzing user workflows, identifying patterns, and evaluating emerging technologies.
  • Partner with internal product teams to identify shared needs and design reusable platform capabilities.
  • Prioritize and sequence roadmap initiatives, balancing technical risk, business value, and phased delivery approaches.
  • Collaborate closely with engineering to guide architectural decisions, platform investments, and execution planning.
  • Communicate platform strategy, trade-offs, and impact clearly to leadership and cross-functional stakeholders.
  • Manage dependencies across product teams, integrations, and data contracts to ensure cohesive platform evolution.

Requirements

You are a strong product leader with experience building or scaling technical, platform-oriented products in complex environments.

  • 5+ years of product management experience, ideally in platform, infrastructure, or integration-heavy product areas.
  • Strong ability to define reusable capabilities rather than solving isolated feature requests.
  • Proven experience driving complex, cross-functional initiatives from discovery through delivery.
  • Ability to operate in ambiguous environments and bring structure, clarity, and prioritization.
  • Strong collaboration skills with engineering teams and comfort discussing technical trade-offs and system design.
  • Excellent communication and storytelling skills, especially in translating technical work into business value.
  • Experience working with APIs, integration systems, and understanding downstream consumer needs.
  • Ability to interpret technical documentation, API contracts, and system design discussions.
  • Data-informed mindset with experience using observability or monitoring tools (e.g., Grafana).
  • Strong judgment in balancing foundational investment versus speed of delivery.

Benefits

  • Competitive compensation aligned with experience
  • Remote-friendly work setup in India
  • Opportunity to shape a critical platform used across multiple product areas
  • High-impact role with strong ownership and autonomy
  • Collaborative environment working closely with engineering and product leadership
  • Exposure to complex platform architecture and scalable system design
  • Growth opportunities in a fast-evolving product organization
  • Inclusive and cross-functional team culture

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Manager - Product Analytics (Individual Contributor)
description: Organizations everywhere struggle under the crushing costs and complexities of “solutions” that promise to simplify their lives. To create a better experience for their customers and employees. To help them grow. Software is a choice that can make or break a business. Create better or worse experiences. Propel or throttle growth. Business software has become a blocker instead of ways to get work done.There’s another option. Freshworks With a fresh vision for how the world works.At Freshworks, we build uncomplicated service software that delivers exceptional customer and employee experiences. Our enterprise-grade solutions are powerful, yet easy to use, and quick to deliver results. Our people-first approach to AI eliminates friction, making employees more effective and organizations more productive. Over 72,000 companies, including Bridgestone, New Balance, Nucor, S&P Global, and Sony Music, trust Freshworks customer experience (CX) and employee experience (EX) software to fuel customer loyalty and service efficiency. And, over 4,500 Freshworks employees make this possible, all around the world.Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.Job DescriptionWe are looking for a Senior Manager – Product Analytics (Individual Contributor) to serve as the hands-on Product Analytics Lead for one of our Product Verticals, owning product analytics end-to-end and shaping product strategy through data. This is a senior IC role for an analytics leader who thrives in ambiguity, partners deeply with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Design, and influences product roadmap decisions with strong analytical rigor. You will work with complex product telemetry, customer behavior, and business data to generate actionable insights that improve customer value and business outcomes, in close collaboration with Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams. You will also mentor 2–4 product analysts, acting as a subject-matter expert and raising the bar for analytics quality, experimentation, and storytelling across the organization.The Impact You Will CreateShape Product Strategy: You will own end-to-end product analytics for a dedicated vertical, transforming complex product telemetry and customer behavior data into actionable insights that directly dictate the product roadmap.Define the North Star: You will establish, evolve, and own the core metrics and KPIs that align Product, Engineering, Marketing, and GTM teams around customer value and business outcomes.Champion an Experimentation Culture: You will lead rigorous A/B testing initiatives from hypothesis to executive interpretation, embedding a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across all product teams.Elevate the Analytics Standard: Acting as a subject-matter expert, you will mentor 2-4 product analysts and raise the organizational bar for data integrity, advanced statistical analysis, and compelling executive storytelling.Responsibilities Own end-to-end product analytics for a Product Vertical with high autonomy and ownership Partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, and Design leaders to influence product strategy and roadmap decisions Collaborate with Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams to support growth, adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives through data Translate ambiguous business and product questions across teams into structured analytical problem statements Define, evolve, and own north-star metrics and product KPIs aligned to customer and business outcomes Act as the product analytics owner for instrumentation and telemetry, driving event design, coverage, and data quality Work with Engineering, TPMs, and Data Engineering to ensure scalable, reliable, and trusted data foundations Lead A/B testing and experimentation initiatives from hypothesis to analysis and interpretation Apply statistical techniques (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) to inform decisions Perform deep-dive analyses on large, complex datasets to uncover root causes and high-impact opportunities Build and maintain analytics products such as dashboards, frameworks, and self-serve insights for product and business stakeholders Communicate insights through clear, compelling narratives and executive-ready presentations to senior leadership Mentor 2–4 junior product analysts, providing guidance on analysis design, experimentation, and storytelling Champion best practices in analytics, including data integrity, validation, documentation, and reproducibility Foster a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across product teamsQualificationsQualifications & SkillsCore Experience10+ years of total experience in analytics or data science, with 5–7+ years of hands-on product analytics experience.Deep domain expertise in B2B SaaS environments is highly preferred.Technical & Data StackData Querying & Scripting: Expert proficiency in SQL for managing large, complex datasets, paired with strong hands-on Python skills for exploratory, statistical, and predictive analysis.Data Visualization: Proven experience building and maintaining self-serve analytics products and dashboards using Power BI (or similar tools).AI/GenAI Leverage: Exposure to utilizing AI and GenAI tools to enhance and accelerate analytics workflows.Analytical Methodologies & Product ThinkingAdvanced Experimentation: Deep understanding of statistical methods (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) and A/B testing design.SaaS Product Metrics: Expertise in product telemetry, event instrumentation design, funnel analysis, and cohort-based lifecycle analysis.Product Sense: Strong business orientation with the ability to connect raw data insights directly to customer adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives.Leadership & CommunicationExecutive Storytelling: Excellent communication skills with a proven track record of translating ambiguous data into clear narratives and executive-ready presentations.Strategic Influence: Proven ability to operate independently in ambiguous environments and influence senior product, engineering, and business leadership.Mentorship: Experience guiding and mentoring junior analysts in analysis design, data validation, and storytelling best practices.Additional InformationAt Freshworks, we have fostered an environment that enables everyone to find their true potential, purpose, and passion, welcoming colleagues of all backgrounds, genders, sexual orientations, religions, and ethnicities. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and believe that diversity in the workplace creates a more vibrant, richer environment that boosts the goals of our employees, communities, and business. Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.

To help them grow. Software is a choice that can make or break a business. Create better or worse experiences. Propel or throttle growth. Business software has become a blocker instead of ways to get work done.



There’s another option. Freshworks With a fresh vision for how the world works.



At Freshworks, we build uncomplicated service software that delivers exceptional customer and employee experiences. Our enterprise-grade solutions are powerful, yet easy to use, and quick to deliver results. Our people-first approach to AI eliminates friction, making employees more effective and organizations more productive. Over 72,000 companies, including Bridgestone, New Balance, Nucor, S&P Global, and Sony Music, trust Freshworks customer experience (CX) and employee experience (EX) software to fuel customer loyalty and service efficiency. And, over 4,500 Freshworks employees make this possible, all around the world.



Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.



Job Description

We are looking for a Senior Manager – Product Analytics (Individual Contributor) to serve as the hands-on Product Analytics Lead for one of our Product Verticals, owning product analytics end-to-end and shaping product strategy through data. This is a senior IC role for an analytics leader who thrives in ambiguity, partners deeply with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Design, and influences product roadmap decisions with strong analytical rigor. You will work with complex product telemetry, customer behavior, and business data to generate actionable insights that improve customer value and business outcomes, in close collaboration with Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams. You will also mentor 2–4 product analysts, acting as a subject-matter expert and raising the bar for analytics quality, experimentation, and storytelling across the organization.

The Impact You Will Create

  • Shape Product Strategy: You will own end-to-end product analytics for a dedicated vertical, transforming complex product telemetry and customer behavior data into actionable insights that directly dictate the product roadmap.

  • Define the North Star: You will establish, evolve, and own the core metrics and KPIs that align Product, Engineering, Marketing, and GTM teams around customer value and business outcomes.

  • Champion an Experimentation Culture: You will lead rigorous A/B testing initiatives from hypothesis to executive interpretation, embedding a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across all product teams.

  • Elevate the Analytics Standard: Acting as a subject-matter expert, you will mentor 2-4 product analysts and raise the organizational bar for data integrity, advanced statistical analysis, and compelling executive storytelling.

Responsibilities

  • Own end-to-end product analytics for a Product Vertical with high autonomy and ownership
  • Partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, and Design leaders to influence product strategy and roadmap decisions
  • Collaborate with Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams to support growth, adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives through data
  • Translate ambiguous business and product questions across teams into structured analytical problem statements
  • Define, evolve, and own north-star metrics and product KPIs aligned to customer and business outcomes
  • Act as the product analytics owner for instrumentation and telemetry, driving event design, coverage, and data quality
  • Work with Engineering, TPMs, and Data Engineering to ensure scalable, reliable, and trusted data foundations
  • Lead A/B testing and experimentation initiatives from hypothesis to analysis and interpretation
  • Apply statistical techniques (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) to inform decisions
  • Perform deep-dive analyses on large, complex datasets to uncover root causes and high-impact opportunities
  • Build and maintain analytics products such as dashboards, frameworks, and self-serve insights for product and business stakeholders
  • Communicate insights through clear, compelling narratives and executive-ready presentations to senior leadership
  • Mentor 2–4 junior product analysts, providing guidance on analysis design, experimentation, and storytelling
  • Champion best practices in analytics, including data integrity, validation, documentation, and reproducibility
  • Foster a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across product teams

Qualifications

Qualifications & Skills

Core Experience

  • 10+ years of total experience in analytics or data science, with 5–7+ years of hands-on product analytics experience.

  • Deep domain expertise in B2B SaaS environments is highly preferred.

Technical & Data Stack

  • Data Querying & Scripting: Expert proficiency in SQL for managing large, complex datasets, paired with strong hands-on Python skills for exploratory, statistical, and predictive analysis.

  • Data Visualization: Proven experience building and maintaining self-serve analytics products and dashboards using Power BI (or similar tools).

  • AI/GenAI Leverage: Exposure to utilizing AI and GenAI tools to enhance and accelerate analytics workflows.

Analytical Methodologies & Product Thinking

  • Advanced Experimentation: Deep understanding of statistical methods (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) and A/B testing design.

  • SaaS Product Metrics: Expertise in product telemetry, event instrumentation design, funnel analysis, and cohort-based lifecycle analysis.

  • Product Sense: Strong business orientation with the ability to connect raw data insights directly to customer adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives.

Leadership & Communication

  • Executive Storytelling: Excellent communication skills with a proven track record of translating ambiguous data into clear narratives and executive-ready presentations.

  • Strategic Influence: Proven ability to operate independently in ambiguous environments and influence senior product, engineering, and business leadership.

  • Mentorship: Experience guiding and mentoring junior analysts in analysis design, data validation, and storytelling best practices.



Additional Information

At Freshworks, we have fostered an environment that enables everyone to find their true potential, purpose, and passion, welcoming colleagues of all backgrounds, genders, sexual orientations, religions, and ethnicities. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and believe that diversity in the workplace creates a more vibrant, richer environment that boosts the goals of our employees, communities, and business. Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.

descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Honda Motor Co., Ltd., Japan is establishing a new Digital Innovation entity in India to accelerate growth through customer intelligence, digital platforms, connected experiences, and next-generation mobility solutions.


Headquartered in Bengaluru, this organization will combine Honda’s global scale with India’s technology ecosystem to build capabilities that directly influence product strategy, customer growth, and new business creation across India and other high-growth markets.


Role Description

We are looking for a Senior Product Manager – Digital Platforms to lead the strategy and execution of customer-facing digital products across mobile apps, web platforms, and future connected services.


You will own the roadmap and evolution of products that improve ownership experience, service engagement, customer retention, and lifecycle value. This role requires someone who can translate real customer needs and Honda business operations into practical, high-impact digital solutions. You will work at the intersection of product, customer experience, operations, and business growth.


Key Responsibilities

  • Own end-to-end product vision for mobile apps, web platforms, and related digital products
  • Define product roadmap aligned with Honda motorcycle and automobile business priorities
  • Translate customer pain points, operational realities, and business needs into product requirements and user stories
  • Drive feature development across the customer lifecycle: purchase → onboarding → usage → service → loyalty
  • Prioritize backlog and work closely with engineering teams for timely delivery
  • Improve customer engagement, retention, service usage, and digital adoption metrics
  • Partner with Sales, Service, Marketing, UX, and Analytics teams to deliver high-value features
  • Validate new concepts through pilots, field feedback, and user testing
  • Monitor product KPIs and continuously optimize journeys and platform performance
  • Act as the voice of business operations and customers in product decisions


Qualifications

Required

  • 8+ years of experience in Program Management, Project Management, Innovation, Strategy Execution, or related roles
  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business or a relevant field
  • Proven experience managing complex cross-functional programs
  • Experience working with startups, incubators, accelerators, or external partners
  • Strong planning, prioritization, and stakeholder management skills
  • Ability to drive execution in fast-moving and ambiguous environments
  • Strong communication and presentation skills


Preferred

  • Prior experience in automotive, mobility, retail, fintech, or consumer digital platforms
  • Exposure to customer lifecycle products, CRM, loyalty, or service platforms
  • Experience with Agile / Scrum product delivery models
  • Field experience in sales, service, dealer network, or customer operations is highly valued
  • Familiarity with app analytics and experimentation tools


Why join

  • Own digital products with direct customer and business impact
  • Build platforms serving millions of users
  • Work across mobility, customer experience, and growth initiatives
  • Strong long-term career growth potential

title: Senior Product Manager
description: Senior Product Manager - MediaSabre’s media technology platform powers the marketing campaigns that travel suppliers purchase across Sabre’s proprietary channels. With our industry‑leading Air, Lodging, Ground, and Sea businesses, along with a growing partner ecosystem, Sabre continues to invest in expanding its media offerings and reach.The Media Product team works closely with customers, commercial teams, and engineering partners to deliver impactful, measurable media solutions. This role is ideal for a product manager who enjoys working in a fast‑moving environment, translating business needs into product capabilities, and learning the end‑to‑end dynamics of media, monetization, and performance measurement.ResponsibilitiesContribute to the execution of the media product roadmap in alignment with strategic and commercial goalsPartner closely with the Media Commercial team to understand customer needs, market feedback, and go‑to‑market prioritiesTranslate product requirements into clear user stories and acceptance criteria to support engineering deliveryCollaborate with Product teams across Agent, Airline, and Corporate channels to support media display, attribution, and conversion tracking capabilitiesSupport Sales and Account teams with customer discussions, demos, and product collateral, and incorporate feedback into product planningHelp track product performance and customer outcomes through dashboards, reporting, and analysisParticipate in backlog prioritization, sprint planning, and ongoing delivery activities with engineering teamsRequirementsExperience in Product Management, product development, or delivery rolesExposure to digital media, advertising platforms, or consumer‑facing products is preferredStrong communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate requirements and product valueExperience working with cross‑functional stakeholders across product, engineering, and commercial teamsComfort operating in environments with evolving requirements and multiple dependenciesAnalytical mindset with interest in using data to inform product decisionsInterest in media, advertising, and the technology that supports monetization and measurementAbility to adapt to a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities.
descriptionHtml:
Senior Product Manager - Media

Sabre’s media technology platform powers the marketing campaigns that travel suppliers purchase across Sabre’s proprietary channels. With our industry‑leading Air, Lodging, Ground, and Sea businesses, along with a growing partner ecosystem, Sabre continues to invest in expanding its media offerings and reach.

The Media Product team works closely with customers, commercial teams, and engineering partners to deliver impactful, measurable media solutions. This role is ideal for a product manager who enjoys working in a fast‑moving environment, translating business needs into product capabilities, and learning the end‑to‑end dynamics of media, monetization, and performance measurement.

Responsibilities

  • Contribute to the execution of the media product roadmap in alignment with strategic and commercial goals
  • Partner closely with the Media Commercial team to understand customer needs, market feedback, and go‑to‑market priorities
  • Translate product requirements into clear user stories and acceptance criteria to support engineering delivery
  • Collaborate with Product teams across Agent, Airline, and Corporate channels to support media display, attribution, and conversion tracking capabilities
  • Support Sales and Account teams with customer discussions, demos, and product collateral, and incorporate feedback into product planning
  • Help track product performance and customer outcomes through dashboards, reporting, and analysis
  • Participate in backlog prioritization, sprint planning, and ongoing delivery activities with engineering teams

Requirements

  • Experience in Product Management, product development, or delivery roles
  • Exposure to digital media, advertising platforms, or consumer‑facing products is preferred
  • Strong communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate requirements and product value
  • Experience working with cross‑functional stakeholders across product, engineering, and commercial teams
  • Comfort operating in environments with evolving requirements and multiple dependencies
  • Analytical mindset with interest in using data to inform product decisions
  • Interest in media, advertising, and the technology that supports monetization and measurement
  • Ability to adapt to a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities.

descriptionHtml:
Weave is a company with a tight product-market fit and a customer-obsessed culture. The Product Team at Weave operates under a development model in which each product manager is expected to lead their team through the stages of product development: discovery, execution, go-to-market, and iteration. We strive for high autonomy and high alignment through clear goals, outcome-based planning, and tight collaboration with engineering, design, support, and GTM teams.

In this role, the Senior Product Manager, Scheduling Platform, will own one of Weave's most strategic product areas: the platform capabilities that power scheduling across our products and workflows. This role focuses on improving the flexibility, reliability, and scalability of our scheduling foundation while defining and enabling new capabilities that support multiple teams, products, and use cases. Scheduling sits at the center of Weave’s product experience. The platform that powers it directly impacts our ability to deliver customer value, support integrations, and expand into more advanced workflows. The right candidate will lead the next phase of platform improvements to make scheduling easier to build on and integrate with across the business.

This position will report to the Director of Product Management

This position will be remote

What You Will Own

  • Own the product strategy, roadmap, and execution for Weave’s Scheduling Platform
  • Lead product discovery for platform-related problems by understanding how users solve problems today, exploring emerging technologies, and identifying where new capabilities can meaningfully improve outcomes for Weave customers
  • Partner with internal product teams (consumers) to understand their needs and identify common opportunities for new platform capabilities
  • Prioritize the platform roadmap and drive execution, making clear calls on risk, sequencing, and business value, and break work into staged deliveries that provide early value while progressing toward longer-term platform improvements
  • Partner closely with engineering to guide platform investments and phased execution
  • Communicate platform decisions, their risks, and business impact to leadership and cross-functional partners
  • Partner with integrations, feature teams, and other product areas to manage dependencies, data contracts, and shared platform evolution

What You Will Need to Accomplish the Job

  • 5+ years of product management experience, ideally with meaningful ownership of a platform, infrastructure-adjacent, integration-heavy, or otherwise technical product area
  • Strong product judgment and the ability to define reusable capabilities rather than just fulfill one-off requests
  • Ability to operate independently, make sound decisions, and create clarity in ambiguous environments
  • Strong execution skills and a track record of driving complex cross-functional work from problem definition through delivery
  • Ability to partner credibly with senior engineers on technical tradeoffs and long-term platform decisions
  • Strong communication and storytelling skills, including the ability to explain why technical platform work matters to the business
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with engineering, design, analytics, support, go-to-market teams, and peer product managers
  • Good judgment on when to invest in durable foundations and when to avoid overbuilding
  • Familiarity with API design and API contract thinking
  • Familiarity with integration constraints and the needs of downstream consumers
  • Comfort reading API documentation, technical specs, and system design discussions
  • Ability to use data and observability tools to understand platform health and behavior, such as Grafana

What Will Make Us Love You

  • You bring strong empathy for platform consumers, informed by prior ownership of customer-facing features
  • Experience with scheduling, booking, calendar, recurrence, availability, or workflow systems
  • Experience with integration-heavy products that span multiple systems or data sources
  • Experience in healthcare technology or similarly operationally complex and compliance environments
  • Familiarity with AI-powered products and workflows (e.g., generative AI, agentic systems) and how they can be applied to improve customer-facing experiences
  • Familiarity with data pipelines and ETL concepts
  • Familiarity with distributed systems concepts and tradeoffs
  • Familiarity with eventual consistency and data reliability concerns

At Weave, we use Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to help us work more efficiently and create a smoother candidate experience. AI may assist with things like writing job descriptions, scheduling interviews, or reviewing applications against job-related criteria. For additional information, please review the External AI Policy Statement available on our Careers page.

Weave is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where all individuals are valued and supported. We welcome anyone who is hungry to learn, problem-solve and progress regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

All official correspondence will occur through Weave branded email. We will never ask you to share bank account information, cash a check from us, or purchase software or equipment as part of your interview or hiring process.

descriptionHtml:
About Wati

Started as a WhatsApp team inbox in 2020, Wati has evolved into a full revenue orchestration system that goes beyond a single platform. We empower businesses that sell, support, and grow through conversations by observing customer intent in real-time, deciding the next best revenue action, and executing it seamlessly across marketing, sales, and support—all within WhatsApp and connected messaging channels.

Our Platform & AI Capabilities

Wati is designed for scalability and intelligence. Our AI-native platform simplifies complex customer communication operations through a unified inbox, a robust multi-channel messaging infrastructure, and no-code automation. At the heart of our solution is Astra, our intelligent AI layer, which helps you create AI Agents for all customer interactions and all your messaging platforms. By integrating AI agents into the ecosystem, we enable businesses of all sizes to deliver measurable ROI and build deeper customer relationships.

Our Backing & Partnerships

Trusted by over 16,000 customers across 190+ countries, Wati is proudly backed by world-class investors including Tiger Global, Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and Shopify. As a Premium-tier Partner of Meta and Google, we maintain the highest standards of platform excellence and integration.

About The Role

Wati is growing rapidly and we are looking to hire a Senior Product Manager to help scale our product offerings and work with our product and engineering teams to develop and execute our shared product vision. We are looking for extremely entrepreneurial PMs who value moving quickly.

If you have demonstrated experience building APIs & MCPs, understand developer communities, have empathy for both consumers and businesses and want to unlock the potential for messaging to serve millions of customers, we would be excited to hear from you.

This will be an opportunity for you to build experiences that thousands of small businesses use on a daily basis, and to be a key part of this high growth journey.

What you'll do:

  • Own the product strategy and roadmap for building native integrations, strong APIs and an Agent friendly platform
  • Identify high-impact integration and ecosystem opportunities by analyzing developer needs, Agent behaviours, partner platforms, and competitive landscapes
  • Work closely with engineering to define APIs, SDKs, auth, and extensibility primitives that scale and are easy to adopt
  • Build a product-led partnerships strategy that turns integrations into a repeatable demand and revenue channel
  • Lead 0→1 and early-scale experiments for new integrations, marketplaces and communities; measuring adoption, activation, and revenue impact
  • Collaborate with sales, partnership and marketing teams to launch, position, and grow integrations and drive adoption amongst developer communities
  • Synthesize learnings and present clear recommendations to leadership

Requirements

  • Experience as a Senior Product Manager at a scale or growth-stage company, ideally on developer, platform, or ecosystem products
  • Strong technical product judgment; comfortable working with AI platforms, APIs, MCPs, integrations, LLM tooling, and developer and agentic workflows
  • Track record of launching new platforms and iterating based on real usage and data
  • Ability to operate with ambiguity while building systems that scale
  • Data-driven mindset with clear communication and stakeholder management skills

Nice to have:

  • Experience at a startup or high-growth scaleup
  • Coding or SQL experience — you don't need to ship code, but you can read it and get your own answers from data
  • Familiarity with the messaging, CPaaS, or WhatsApp ecosystem
  • Bachelor's in Computer Science or a related technical field

descriptionHtml:
At Dario, Every Day is a New Opportunity to Make a Difference.

We are on a mission to make better health easy. Every day our employees contribute to this mission and help hundreds of thousands of people around the globe improve their health. How cool is that? We are looking for passionate, smart, and collaborative people who have a desire to do something meaningful and impactful in their career.


We are looking for a strong Senior Product Manager who combines strategic thinking, healthcare market expertise, data-driven decision-making, and hands-on execution leadership.

This role owns both product direction and measurable business impact — including engagement, retention, and revenue contribution.


Only candidates currently residing in Gurugram, or those willing to relocate, will be considered


What You Will Do

Strategic Product Leadership

  • Define and drive product strategy aligned with company growth objectives.
  • Identify opportunities to increase member engagement, retention, and platform utilization.
  • Analyze healthcare and digital health market trends to uncover growth and differentiation opportunities.
  • Build and maintain a clear, outcome-driven roadmap that balances innovation, scalability, and revenue impact.

Engagement & Revenue Ownership

  • Define and track KPIs tied to engagement, activation, retention, clinical outcomes, and revenue contribution.
  • Drive initiatives that improve member stickiness and long-term value.
  • Partner with business stakeholders to ensure product decisions support monetization models and expansion opportunities.
  • Continuously optimize product performance based on measurable ROI.

Data-Driven Decision Making

  • Use analytics, experimentation, and market insights to validate hypotheses and prioritize investments.
  • Lead structured discovery processes grounded in quantitative and qualitative data.
  • Make informed trade-offs based on business impact and long-term value creation.

Engineering Partnership & Execution

  • Lead planning, prioritization, and execution within a dedicated scrum team.
  • Own backlog strategy, sprint goals, and release planning.
  • Work closely with engineering, data science, and design to deliver AI/ML and GenAI-powered capabilities.
  • Balance speed, technical scalability, and business value in execution decisions.
  • Ensure solutions align with long-term platform and shared services strategy.

AI & Platform Innovation

  • Drive responsible integration of AI and Generative AI into scalable healthcare experiences.
  • Translate advanced AI capabilities (personalization, predictive insights, conversational AI) into features that increase engagement and outcomes.
  • Contribute to reusable platform capabilities across conditions and markets.

Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Align engineering, clinical, compliance, and business stakeholders around clear priorities and measurable outcomes.
  • Communicate strategy, roadmap, and impact to senior leadership.
  • Ensure compliance with regulatory frameworks (HIPAA, FDA, data privacy).


Qualifications


  • 8–10 years of product management experience, ideally in digital health, healthcare SaaS, or regulated industries.
  • Experience building and scaling mobile applications (iOS and/or Android), with strong understanding of mobile product metrics, user experience, and release cycles.
  • Proven track record of driving measurable improvements in engagement, retention, and revenue.
  • Strong experience shaping product strategy based on market analysis and performance data.
  • Solid understanding of healthcare ecosystem dynamics and digital health trends.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead scrum teams and own delivery end-to-end.
  • Experience delivering AI/ML-driven capabilities in collaboration with engineering and data science.
  • Strong analytical mindset with ownership of KPIs and business outcomes.
  • Ability to confidently engage with business stakeholders while working closely with engineering.
  • Excellent English communication skills (verbal and written).
  • Self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, evolving environments.


***DarioHealth promotes diversity of thought, culture and background, which connects the entire Dario team. We believe that every member on our team enriches our diversity by exposing us to a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the world, identify challenges, and to discover, design and deliver solutions. We are passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable working and learning environments for all people, and do not discriminate against any employee or job candidate. ***

title: Senior Product Manager
description: Alation is pioneering the next era of data intelligence — powered by AI agents and trusted data. Organizations around the world rely on Alation to drive self-service analytics, cloud transformation, data governance, and AI innovation. Our technology helps enterprises connect people and data to make faster, smarter, and more confident decisions. With more than $340M in funding – valued at over $1.7 billion and more than 650 customers, including 40% of the Fortune 100.As data becomes the foundation of every business, Alation is leading the way in helping organizations unlock their full potential. By bringing intelligence, automation, and trust to every interaction, we empower customers to scale data-driven innovation with confidence and speed.Joining Alation means being part of a winning team shaping the future of AI-powered data intelligence. We move fast, think big, and deliver results together.Job DescriptionAlation’s vision is to deliver the most trusted and actionable data intelligence platform. The lineage graph is one of the most critical assets in pursuit of that vision. It must be accurate, performant, stable, and deeply interoperable—powering both customer-facing lineage experiences and an entire ecosystem of metadata-driven applications, including impact analysis, root cause diagnosis, curation propagation, and automation workflows.The lineage graph is a foundational capability within Alation’s Data Intelligence Platform. It underpins trust, enables governance at scale, and powers a growing set of customer-facing and internal applications—from impact analysis to curation automation.We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to own this lineage platform end to end. This role is for a product leader who is comfortable operating at the intersection of platform infrastructure, customer outcomes, and long-term technical strategy—and who wants to raise the standard for product thinking and execution across the organization.What You'll Be DoingOwn the vision, strategy, and delivery of Alation’s lineage graph as a core platform capability. Ensure the lineage graph is stable, trustworthy, and consumable across UI, APIs, and external systems. Partner with customers and other squads to directly or indirectly enable lineage-powered experiences and applications across agentic governance and analytics workflows. Partner deeply with engineering to evolve scalable graph infrastructure and set quality standards. Raise the bar for product clarity, execution quality, and cross-team alignment.You Should Have5–10+ years of product experience, ideally building platform, infrastructure, or deeply technical data systems. Demonstrated ability to own complex graphs, metadata systems, data pipelines, or large-scale distributed systems. Strength in systems thinking and architecture-level problem solving. Ability to navigate ambiguity, create alignment, and distill complex technical domains into crisp decision frameworks. Exceptional communication skills; ability to influence across engineering, leadership, and customers. Experience working with enterprise data ecosystems (data lakes, warehouses, ETL/ELT, BI, orchestration tools) is strongly preferred. A high standard for execution, quality, and craft—both in the product and in how the org operates.Alation, Inc. is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regards to that individual’s race, color, religion or creed, national origin or ancestry, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, veteran status, genetic information, ethnicity, citizenship, or any other characteristic protected by law. The Company will strive to provide reasonable accommodations to permit qualified applicants who have a need for an accommodation to participate in the hiring process (e.g., accommodations for a job interview) if so requested. This company participates in E-Verify. Click on any of the links below to view or print the full poster.  E-Verify and Right to Work.
descriptionHtml:
Alation is pioneering the next era of data intelligence — powered by AI agents and trusted data. Organizations around the world rely on Alation to drive self-service analytics, cloud transformation, data governance, and AI innovation. Our technology helps enterprises connect people and data to make faster, smarter, and more confident decisions. With more than $340M in funding – valued at over $1.7 billion and more than 650 customers, including 40% of the Fortune 100.

As data becomes the foundation of every business, Alation is leading the way in helping organizations unlock their full potential. By bringing intelligence, automation, and trust to every interaction, we empower customers to scale data-driven innovation with confidence and speed.

Joining Alation means being part of a winning team shaping the future of AI-powered data intelligence. We move fast, think big, and deliver results together.

Job Description

Alation’s vision is to deliver the most trusted and actionable data intelligence platform. The lineage graph is one of the most critical assets in pursuit of that vision. It must be accurate, performant, stable, and deeply interoperable—powering both customer-facing lineage experiences and an entire ecosystem of metadata-driven applications, including impact analysis, root cause diagnosis, curation propagation, and automation workflows.

The lineage graph is a foundational capability within Alation’s Data Intelligence Platform. It underpins trust, enables governance at scale, and powers a growing set of customer-facing and internal applications—from impact analysis to curation automation.

We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to own this lineage platform end to end. This role is for a product leader who is comfortable operating at the intersection of platform infrastructure, customer outcomes, and long-term technical strategy—and who wants to raise the standard for product thinking and execution across the organization.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Own the vision, strategy, and delivery of Alation’s lineage graph as a core platform capability.
  • Ensure the lineage graph is stable, trustworthy, and consumable across UI, APIs, and external systems.
  • Partner with customers and other squads to directly or indirectly enable lineage-powered experiences and applications across agentic governance and analytics workflows.
  • Partner deeply with engineering to evolve scalable graph infrastructure and set quality standards.
  • Raise the bar for product clarity, execution quality, and cross-team alignment.

You Should Have

  • 5–10+ years of product experience, ideally building platform, infrastructure, or deeply technical data systems.
  • Demonstrated ability to own complex graphs, metadata systems, data pipelines, or large-scale distributed systems.
  • Strength in systems thinking and architecture-level problem solving.
  • Ability to navigate ambiguity, create alignment, and distill complex technical domains into crisp decision frameworks.
  • Exceptional communication skills; ability to influence across engineering, leadership, and customers.
  • Experience working with enterprise data ecosystems (data lakes, warehouses, ETL/ELT, BI, orchestration tools) is strongly preferred.
  • A high standard for execution, quality, and craft—both in the product and in how the org operates.

Alation, Inc. is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regards to that individual’s race, color, religion or creed, national origin or ancestry, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, veteran status, genetic information, ethnicity, citizenship, or any other characteristic protected by law.

The Company will strive to provide reasonable accommodations to permit qualified applicants who have a need for an accommodation to participate in the hiring process (e.g., accommodations for a job interview) if so requested.

This company participates in E-Verify. Click on any of the links below to view or print the full poster. E-Verify and Right to Work.

title: Senior Product Manager - AI Platform

This is a hands-on product role that operates at the intersection of AI engineering, enterprise software, and capital program workflows. You will partner closely with engineering, data science, and design teams to define, build, and ship AI capabilities that are safe, measurable, and impactful in enterprise environments. Unlike the Director role which is focused on strategy, vision, and stakeholder leadership, this role is about deep execution. You will own specific product areas end to end, write detailed requirements, drive sprint-level delivery, and ensure that AI products meet well-defined quality and safety standards before reaching customers. Advanced degree in a relevant discipline is an advantage.Prior Experience7 to 10 years of experience in product management, with at least 2 to 3 years owning AI or machine learning product features in a B2B SaaS or enterprise software environment.Demonstrated experience shipping AI features to production in enterprise contexts, including agents, copilots, predictive models, or recommendation systems.Hands-on experience defining evaluation criteria and running evals for AI features, including benchmark design, human evaluation coordination, and production quality tracking.Experience working directly with data science and machine learning engineering teams on model prototyping, prompt iteration, or deployment workflows.Background in infrastructure, construction, capital program management, or other complex enterprise domains is a strong advantage.About AurigoAurigo provides AI-native platforms that help capital owners connect planning, construction, and operations in a single environment, improving decision-making and execution. Trusted by over 300 organizations across federal, state, and local government, manufacturing, data centers, energy and utilities, and life sciences, we manage more than $450 billion in capital programs across 40,000+ projects in North America. If you're excited to join a fast-growing company and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry to solve real-world challenges, let's connect.
descriptionHtml:
About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Product Manager for our AI Platform to drive the execution and delivery of Aurigo's proprietary AI foundation model, agent framework, and the AI platform layer that powers our products. This is a hands-on product role that operates at the intersection of AI engineering, enterprise software, and capital program workflows. You will partner closely with engineering, data science, and design teams to define, build, and ship AI capabilities that are safe, measurable, and impactful in enterprise environments. Unlike the Director role which is focused on strategy, vision, and stakeholder leadership, this role is about deep execution. You will own specific product areas end to end, write detailed requirements, drive sprint-level delivery, and ensure that AI products meet well-defined quality and safety standards before reaching customers. You will also engage with customers and internal product teams to ground your decisions in real-world use cases.


Roles and Responsibilities

  • Own and execute the product roadmap for assigned areas of the AI platform, including the ingestion and context layer, orchestration layer, agent framework components, and the Copilot experience across all product lines.
  • Write detailed product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria for AI features, and drive sprint planning, backlog grooming, and delivery tracking in close partnership with engineering and machine learning teams.
  • Define and maintain the evaluation framework for assigned AI features including offline evals, benchmark metrics, regression tests, and production monitoring. No agent or model capability ships without defined success criteria.
  • Own the responsible AI execution process at the feature level, including guardrail testing, safety checklists, bias review, and auditability verification before any AI capability reaches customers.
  • Serve as the internal AI platform expert for Senior Product Managers across all product lines, providing guidance on how to correctly integrate, specify, and evaluate AI capabilities within their respective workflows.
  • Contribute to the model management and MCP server roadmap by gathering data on provider performance, cost, and quality, and by identifying integration opportunities and validating tool specifications.
  • Engage with enterprise customers to gather feedback on AI feature performance, understand adoption patterns, and translate insights into roadmap decisions.
  • Monitor the competitive landscape for relevant AI product developments, agent frameworks, and enterprise AI tooling, and bring insights into prioritization discussions.


Soft Skills

  • Technically credible with engineering and data science teams. Can engage substantively on prompt design, evaluation tradeoffs, RAG configurations, and agent behavior without needing to write code.
  • Clear and precise communicator. Able to write requirements, specs, and documentation that engineering teams can act on without repeated clarification.
  • Customer empathy. Listens carefully to enterprise customer feedback and translates nuanced qualitative input into actionable product decisions.
  • High ownership mindset. Takes accountability for feature quality, safety, and customer outcomes, not just delivery of scope.
  • Structured problem-solver. Breaks ambiguous AI product challenges into well-defined hypotheses, experiments, and evaluation plans.
  • Stays current with fast-moving developments in foundation models, agentic architectures, and enterprise AI deployment patterns.


Hard Skills

  • Strong understanding of the AI product development lifecycle including data pipelines, prompt engineering, RAG architectures, fine-tuning, and agentic system design.
  • Experience designing and running AI evaluation frameworks including offline evals, human evaluation pipelines, and production monitoring for AI features.
  • Familiarity with responsible AI principles including human-in-the-loop design, fairness considerations, auditability, and guardrail implementation in enterprise settings.
  • Working knowledge of model context protocol or equivalent tool-use and integration frameworks for agentic systems.
  • Ability to define and track AI-specific product metrics including task completion rates, hallucination rates, model latency, cost per inference, and user satisfaction signals.


Technical Skills

  • Familiarity with major foundation model providers and the practical tradeoffs among them in terms of capability, cost, latency, context window, and data privacy.
  • Working knowledge of vector databases, semantic search, and embedding-based retrieval systems relevant to RAG product design.
  • Familiarity with AI evaluation tooling such as LLM eval libraries, RAGAS, or equivalent frameworks for measuring AI system performance.
  • Understanding of AI orchestration patterns including ReAct loops, multi-agent coordination, and durable workflow execution for long-running agent tasks.
  • Proficiency with standard product management tools alongside AI-specific development and observability platforms.


Educational Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related technical field. Advanced degree in a relevant discipline is an advantage.


Prior Experience

  • 7 to 10 years of experience in product management, with at least 2 to 3 years owning AI or machine learning product features in a B2B SaaS or enterprise software environment.
  • Demonstrated experience shipping AI features to production in enterprise contexts, including agents, copilots, predictive models, or recommendation systems.
  • Hands-on experience defining evaluation criteria and running evals for AI features, including benchmark design, human evaluation coordination, and production quality tracking.
  • Experience working directly with data science and machine learning engineering teams on model prototyping, prompt iteration, or deployment workflows.
  • Background in infrastructure, construction, capital program management, or other complex enterprise domains is a strong advantage.


About Aurigo

Aurigo provides AI-native platforms that help capital owners connect planning, construction, and operations in a single environment, improving decision-making and execution. Trusted by over 300 organizations across federal, state, and local government, manufacturing, data centers, energy and utilities, and life sciences, we manage more than $450 billion in capital programs across 40,000+ projects in North America. Built on 20+ years of domain expertise, Aurigo has received multiple industry awards and has been certified as a Great Place to Work for three consecutive years.


At Aurigo, we go beyond building technology—we shape the future. If you're excited to join a fast-growing company and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry to solve real-world challenges, let's connect.

title: Senior Product Manager - PLG

descriptionHtml:
Wati is the world's leading WhatsApp-first conversational growth platform, empowering businesses to build deeper customer relationships and accelerate revenue growth.

Trusted and loved by over 16,000 customers across 190+ countries, Wati has established itself as the premier choice for businesses leveraging WhatsApp and other messaging channels to connect with customers on their platform of choice.

In a world where real-time interactions drive growth, Wati's story is all about business growth through conversations. At Wati, we believe that every conversation matters, whether it's engaging a first-time prospect, nurturing a qualified lead, or deepening relationships with loyal clients. We transform these conversations into opportunities for building relationships, and strong relationships lead to increased revenue.

Our Platform

The AI-native platform simplifies complex business operations by bringing all customer interactions under one intelligent roof. Designed for scalability and ease of use, our solution delivers a measurable ROI while adapting to businesses of all sizes, from emerging startups to established enterprises.

Our Backing & Partnerships

Wati is proudly backed by world-class investors including Tiger Global, Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and Shopify. As a Premium-tier Partner of Meta, Google, and WhatsApp, we maintain the highest standards of integration and platform excellence.

Role Overview

Wati is experiencing rapid growth and we are looking to hire a Senior Product Manager for our PLG team to own and drive activation, retention & expansion, build a roadmap of experiments and execute quickly to show real business impact. We are looking for extremely entrepreneurial PMs who value moving quickly.

If you have demonstrated experience in growth teams, worked in PLG products and want to unlock the potential for messaging to serve millions of customers, we would be excited to hear from you.

This will be an opportunity for you to build experiences that thousands of small businesses use on a daily basis, and to be a key part of this high growth journey.

What you'll do:

  • Track and benchmark activation, retention, and expansion metrics across customer segments — and use AI to surface patterns and anomalies faster than traditional analysis
  • Hypothesize, test, and iterate on growth experiments; measure their impact and feed learnings back into the roadmap
  • Build and run personalization and nudge systems that use AI to deliver the right message, to the right user, at the right moment in their journey
  • Drive new growth experiments end-to-end — from framing the hypothesis to aligning stakeholders, executing with cross-functional teams, and measuring outcomes
  • Work with domain PMs to connect activation and retention signals to specific use cases, and influence their roadmaps with data-backed recommendations
  • Bring AI into growth workflows — from insight generation and cohort analysis to automated experiment readouts and predictive churn signals
  • Understand the messaging ecosystem, market dynamics, and competitive landscape to inform where growth bets should be placed
  • Collaborate closely with Engineering, Marketing, UX, and Customer teams to move fast and learn faster

Requirements

  • 4-5+ years of experience as a Growth PM or Product Analyst in a B2B SaaS environment
  • Demonstrated experience identifying the levers that move a metric and running rigorous A/B tests
  • Hands-on experience with AI tools for analysis, personalization, or workflow automation — you bring AI into how you work, not just what you ship
  • Strong comfort with data — working directly with raw datasets, writing queries, and using analytics tools like Amplitude, Heap, or Mixpanel
  • Experience designing or influencing personalization systems, behavioral nudges, or lifecycle engagement flows
  • Ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders without direct authority
  • Clear, compelling communication — written, verbal, and in presentations
  • An eye for design — you can spot a friction point in a flow and know what good looks like
  • Comfort holding your own in technical conversations with engineers and data scientists
  • Creative problem-solving grounded in customer empathy, not just funnel metrics

Nice to have:

  • Experience at a startup or high-growth scaleup
  • Coding or SQL experience — you don't need to ship code, but you can read it and get your own answers from data
  • Familiarity with the messaging, CPaaS, or WhatsApp ecosystem
  • Bachelor's in Computer Science or a related technical field

title: Senior Product Manager - Cards
description: About this Role:Cards are the core growth engine for Aspire - driving spend, customer engagement across our platform. Thousands of businesses across Southeast Asia use Aspire cards every day and we’re scaling rapidly to new geographies, currencies, features and customer segments. This requires deep thinking our card products are built - from issuing infrastructure, partner integrations, internal operations, and card propositions.As Senior Product Manager, Cards, you will own the strategy, roadmap and execution of Aspire’s card programs end-to-end. This means shaping our multi-geography and multi-currency product vision; leading integrations with card networks, issuer processors, banking partners; and building internal infrastructure that lets us scale reliably. You’ll work hand-in-hand with engineering, design, operations, compliance and commercial teams to ship products that our customers and partners genuinely rely on.This is a high ownership role at the intersection of product, infrastructure and business.What you’ll do?Own the cards roadmap: Define the product strategy and roadmap for card programs; be accountable for launch and growth performanceBuild and scale issuing infrastructure: Lead integrations with networks, issuers, and processors; ensure reliable authorization, clearing, settlement, and reconciliation at scaleOwn project delivery: Lead complex, cross-functional integrations / initiatives, working collaboratively with product, engineering, operations, compliance and commercial; own delivery timelines and qualityOwn partner relationships: Manage and negotiate with networks, issuers, processors and payment partnersBuild for compliance: Ensure all programs meets the regulatory, scheme and security standards of the markets we operate inBe deeply technical and data-driven: Go deep on technical mechanics of card program ; 1>10) Exposure to multi-geo card programsBackground in engineering, data or fintech
descriptionHtml:
About this Role:


Cards are the core growth engine for Aspire - driving spend, customer engagement across our platform. Thousands of businesses across Southeast Asia use Aspire cards every day and we’re scaling rapidly to new geographies, currencies, features and customer segments. This requires deep thinking our card products are built - from issuing infrastructure, partner integrations, internal operations, and card propositions.

As Senior Product Manager, Cards, you will own the strategy, roadmap and execution of Aspire’s card programs end-to-end. This means shaping our multi-geography and multi-currency product vision; leading integrations with card networks, issuer processors, banking partners; and building internal infrastructure that lets us scale reliably. You’ll work hand-in-hand with engineering, design, operations, compliance and commercial teams to ship products that our customers and partners genuinely rely on.

This is a high ownership role at the intersection of product, infrastructure and business.


What you’ll do?

  • Own the cards roadmap: Define the product strategy and roadmap for card programs; be accountable for launch and growth performance
  • Build and scale issuing infrastructure: Lead integrations with networks, issuers, and processors; ensure reliable authorization, clearing, settlement, and reconciliation at scale
  • Own project delivery: Lead complex, cross-functional integrations / initiatives, working collaboratively with product, engineering, operations, compliance and commercial; own delivery timelines and quality
  • Own partner relationships: Manage and negotiate with networks, issuers, processors and payment partners
  • Build for compliance: Ensure all programs meets the regulatory, scheme and security standards of the markets we operate in
  • Be deeply technical and data-driven: Go deep on technical mechanics of card program ; comfortable on technical with engineering


What we are looking for?

Core experience:

  • 5+ years as a Product Manager, ideally in cards, fintech, or banking infrastructure
  • Track record of owning and scaling a product, not just shipping features


Operator mindset:

  • Sharp problem-solving and attention to detail
  • Bias to action
  • Ability to drive complex, cross-functional initiatives across multiple stakeholders


Technical depth:

  • Strong understanding of system design, SDLC, flows, APIs
  • Able to work closely with Product and Engineering and make informed technical decisions
  • Solid understanding of AI concepts and experience applying AI tools to monitoring, anomaly detection, risk signals, or operational automation.


Communication:

  • Clear, structured communicator with the ability to influence internal and external stakeholders.


Preferred qualifications:

  • Hands-on experience launching or scaling card programs (0>1; 1>10) Exposure to multi-geo card programs
  • Background in engineering, data or fintech

descriptionHtml:
Overview

Senior Staff Product Manager – Virtual Expert Platform

Join Intuit’s Virtual Expert Platform (VEP) team as a Senior Staff Product Manager and shape the future of platform innovation at scale. This is a high-impact role where you will drive the strategy, design, and delivery of platform-critical capabilities that connect millions of consumers, small businesses, and accountants to the right experts across all Intuit product lines.

We are seeking an exceptional platform product manager who brings deep experience in building and scaling mission-critical platforms. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with strong technical acumen, proven ability to drive cross-company adoption, and a track record of delivering enterprise-grade platforms that unlock customer and business value.

Responsibilities

What You’ll Do

  • Own and define the platform vision and strategy: Set a bold, multi-year direction for capabilities in Virtual Expert Platform, ensuring it meets the needs of diverse customers and scales across Intuit’s ecosystem.
  • Drive platform architecture and scale: Partner closely with engineering and architecture to design resilient, extensible, and scalable platform solutions that power core customer and expert experiences.
  • Enable platform adoption and reusability: Deliver services and APIs that are broadly adopted across multiple product lines, accelerating innovation and ensuring consistency in customer experiences.
  • Leverage AI and data at scale: Harness AI/ML and data-driven insights to deliver personalized, proactive, and intelligent experiences across the platform.
  • Execute with impact: Translate vision into strategy and roadmaps, drive prioritization, and deliver measurable outcomes aligned with customer success and company growth.
  • Build organizational alignment: Collaborate with senior executives, cross-functional teams, and business partners to ensure platform initiatives have adoption, visibility, and strategic alignment.
  • Continuously optimize: Improve platform operating models, processes, and governance to maximize scalability, quality, and speed.

Qualifications

What We’re Looking For

  • Proven platform expertise: 8+ years of product management experience with significant time spent on platform products—driving adoption across multiple product lines or business units.
  • Track record of scaling platforms: Demonstrated success in designing and scaling mission-critical, high-availability platforms that power core business functions.
  • Strong technical foundation: Ability to engage deeply with engineering on architecture, APIs, scalability, and integration. Experience with AI/ML-driven products is a strong plus.
  • Customer-first mindset: Passion for understanding customer needs, with a history of translating insights into platform strategies that deliver real value.
  • Business and execution leadership: Data-driven decision-making, excellent prioritization, and a record of delivering impactful results in complex environments.
  • Influential leadership: Strong ability to collaborate, influence, and align executives, engineering, and cross-functional teams around a shared platform vision.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field required; MBA or equivalent experience preferred.

Intuit provides a competitive compensation package with a strong pay for performance rewards approach. This position may be eligible for a cash bonus, equity rewards and benefits, in accordance with our applicable plans and programs (see more about our compensation and benefits at Intuit®: Careers | Benefits). Pay offered is based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and work location. To drive ongoing fair pay for employees, Intuit conducts regular comparisons across categories of ethnicity and gender. The expected base pay range for this position is:
