title: Sr. Product Manager - Workflow Extended
description: About UsHighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. With 250 terabytes of distributed data, 250+ microservices and over 1 million domain names supported, our architecture is built for performance, resilience and long-term scalability.Our peopleWith over 2,000 team members across 10+ countries, HighLevel operates as a global, remote-first organization built for speed and ownership. We value initiative, clarity and execution, creating space for ambitious people to build systems that support millions of businesses worldwide. Here, innovation thrives, ideas are celebrated and people come first, no matter where they call home.Our impactEvery month, HighLevel enables more than 1.5 billion messages, 200 million leads and 20 million conversations for the more than 1 million businesses we support. Behind those numbers are real people building independence, expanding opportunity and creating measurable impact. We’re proud to be a part of that.Learn more about us on our YouTube Channel or Blog PostsAbout The TeamThe Workflows Module is the key component of HighLevel’s automation engine, enabling agencies and businesses to create powerful, automated workflows that enhance efficiency, save time, and improve outcomes. By integrating with multiple product areas within the HighLevel platform and external apps, the Workflows Module empowers users to streamline processes, nurture leads, and manage tasks seamlessly.The Workflows extended team powers the extensibility layer of HighLevel. Integrations are critical to enabling agencies and businesses to connect their external ecosystem - CRMs, email systems, form builders, contact tools, and other SaaS platforms - into HighLevel workflows.This team focuses on building deep, reliable, and scalable integrations that go beyond surface-level triggers and actions. The goal is to reduce framework gaps, improve time-to-ship for integrations, and ensure that integrations meaningfully drive workflow adoption, activation, and retention.About The RoleWe are looking for a Senior Product Manager to join the Workflows Team, with a strong focus on Integrations, Framework Enablement, and Vertical Ownership. This role is ideal for a self-driven individual who is passionate about automation, SaaS ecosystems, and building scalable platform capabilities.As a SPM, you will lead the strategy and execution for integration verticals such as CRMs, Forms, Email Systems, and other key ecosystem partners. You will own these verticals end-to-end - from API evaluation and framework readiness to configuration design, user experience, adoption, performance, and long-term reliability.You will work closely with cross-functional teams including engineering, design, partnerships, and other product managers to deeply understand user pain points and ecosystem opportunities. Your responsibility will be to translate these insights into scalable framework improvements and high-impact integrations that make it easier for users to automate complex business processes.Beyond shipping integrations, you will proactively identify framework gaps before they become delivery bottlenecks, reduce integration time-to-ship, and ensure every integration meaningfully drives workflow usage, customer adoption, and ecosystem growth. You will leverage data, user insights, and structured experimentation to continuously improve integration performance and product impact.Your ResponsibilitiesIntegration & Marketplace Ownership: Own marketplace and integration vertical(s) such as CRMs, Forms, Email Systems, or Contact Platforms end-to-end, including roadmap, delivery, reliability, and adoption. API & Configuration Definition: Review third-party API documentation and define integration configurations including authentication flows (OAuth, API keys), field mapping, data types, webhooks, polling strategies, pagination, rate limits, retries, and UI configuration controls. Framework Gap Identification: Proactively identify missing workflow framework primitives and drive fixes before engineering execution begins to avoid reactive delivery slowdowns. Adoption & Activation: Ensure integrations are deeply leveraged by improving trigger/action quality, importer flows, data parity, and workflow entry points such as hotlinks and breadcrumbs to increase activation and usage. Reliability & Quality: Improve integration reliability, success rates, and execution consistency across triggers and actions. Support Insight to Product Fixes: Analyze integration-related support tickets and ecosystem feedback to identify recurring friction and drive product improvements. Tracking Metrics: Track integration time-to-ship, triggers/actions shipped per sprint, workflow usage tied to integrations, and reliability metrics to continuously improve performance. Documentation & Enablement: Partner with documentation and AI teams to build high-quality help content and structured knowledge for both customers and AI-driven assistance. Staying updated on industry trends in APIs, ecosystem integrations, automation platforms, and no-code/low-code tools to drive innovation within workflows. Your Core SkillsExperience in one or more of the following: Product Management Platform or API-based Products SaaS Integrations or Ecosystem Platforms Passionate about building intuitive and scalable workflow automation solutions. Ability to deeply understand user problems, spot friction in product flows, and make thoughtful trade-offs between power and simplicity. Strong understanding of API concepts including OAuth, REST APIs, webhooks, pagination, rate limits, and data modeling. Ability to deeply understand ecosystem workflows, anticipate framework gaps, and make thoughtful trade-offs between speed and scalability. Comfort with digging into integration reliability metrics, workflow usage data, and support patterns to find actionable insights. Understanding of product development lifecycle and experience working on SaaS platform products. Strong analytical mindset to interpret activation funnels, adoption trends, and execution reliability. Leadership and collaboration skills, with the ability to work cross-functionally and drive platform initiatives forward. Understanding of automation tools and workflow best practices to enhance user experience. Ability to deconstruct complex system interactions and simplify them into intuitive configuration experiences. Familiarity with workflow automation platforms, CRMs, or integration ecosystems is a plus. A systems thinker who thrives in a fast-paced, execution-driven environment. Experience8+ years of Product Management experience ideally in SaaS or automation platforms Proven track record of shipping complex, user-facing features in a fast-paced environment Proven experience owning large-scale, customer-facing product platforms Experience working on workflow automation tools or CRM platforms is a plusDeep Understanding of:Workflow builders, automations and no-code or low-code platforms Product funnels: activation, retention, and reducing time to value Identifying friction points in UX and translating feedback/support data into product decisions Cross-team platform work: enabling other product teams through triggers, actions, and integrations API integrations and ecosystem design principles Workflow builders, automations and no-code or low-code platforms Product funnels: activation, retention, and reducing time to value Data parity, imports, synchronization systems, and reliability engineering Cross-team platform work: enabling other product teams through triggers, actions, and shared primitives Working with engineering on scalable frontend + backend architecture Running feature discovery and validation loops for ecosystem products Partnering closely with design to simplify complex integration setups Writing clear, structured PRDs and communicating roadmap tradeoffs to stakeholders Analyzing data from tools like Superset, Pendo or Tableau to inform product decisions Why Should You Join Us?At HighLevel, we foster an exciting and dynamic work environment driven by a passionate team. We believe in a collective responsibility where no task is considered someone else's job. Our unwavering focus is on providing value to our users, and we achieve this by delivering solutions swiftly through lean principles, allowing us to bring products to market in a matter of weeks rather than quarters. Every good idea is put to the test, ensuring that we maintain a high standard of innovation. We prioritise the well-being of our team, recognizing that by taking care of them, they can better serve our users. We embrace the concept of continuous and iterative improvement, understanding that progress is an ongoing journey. We are also a well funded & profitable company.Join us at High Level, and you will have the opportunity to learn the intricacies of scaling a B2B SaaS startup and develop impactful products that cater to the needs of our customers.Equal Employment Opportunity InformationThe company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. As an employer subject to affirmative action regulations, we invite you to voluntarily provide the following demographic information. This information is used solely for compliance with government recordkeeping, reporting, and other legal requirements. Providing this information is voluntary and refusal to do so will not affect your application status. This data will be kept separate from your application and will not be used in the hiring decision.We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
descriptionHtml:
About Us

HighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.

To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.

Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. With 250 terabytes of distributed data, 250+ microservices and over 1 million domain names supported, our architecture is built for performance, resilience and long-term scalability.

Our people

With over 2,000 team members across 10+ countries, HighLevel operates as a global, remote-first organization built for speed and ownership. We value initiative, clarity and execution, creating space for ambitious people to build systems that support millions of businesses worldwide. Here, innovation thrives, ideas are celebrated and people come first, no matter where they call home.

Our impact

Every month, HighLevel enables more than 1.5 billion messages, 200 million leads and 20 million conversations for the more than 1 million businesses we support. Behind those numbers are real people building independence, expanding opportunity and creating measurable impact. We’re proud to be a part of that.

Learn more about us on our YouTube Channel or Blog Posts

About The Team

The Workflows Module is the key component of HighLevel’s automation engine, enabling agencies and businesses to create powerful, automated workflows that enhance efficiency, save time, and improve outcomes. By integrating with multiple product areas within the HighLevel platform and external apps, the Workflows Module empowers users to streamline processes, nurture leads, and manage tasks seamlessly.

The Workflows extended team powers the extensibility layer of HighLevel. Integrations are critical to enabling agencies and businesses to connect their external ecosystem - CRMs, email systems, form builders, contact tools, and other SaaS platforms - into HighLevel workflows.

This team focuses on building deep, reliable, and scalable integrations that go beyond surface-level triggers and actions. The goal is to reduce framework gaps, improve time-to-ship for integrations, and ensure that integrations meaningfully drive workflow adoption, activation, and retention.

About The Role

We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to join the Workflows Team, with a strong focus on Integrations, Framework Enablement, and Vertical Ownership. This role is ideal for a self-driven individual who is passionate about automation, SaaS ecosystems, and building scalable platform capabilities.

As a SPM, you will lead the strategy and execution for integration verticals such as CRMs, Forms, Email Systems, and other key ecosystem partners. You will own these verticals end-to-end - from API evaluation and framework readiness to configuration design, user experience, adoption, performance, and long-term reliability.

You will work closely with cross-functional teams including engineering, design, partnerships, and other product managers to deeply understand user pain points and ecosystem opportunities. Your responsibility will be to translate these insights into scalable framework improvements and high-impact integrations that make it easier for users to automate complex business processes.

Beyond shipping integrations, you will proactively identify framework gaps before they become delivery bottlenecks, reduce integration time-to-ship, and ensure every integration meaningfully drives workflow usage, customer adoption, and ecosystem growth. You will leverage data, user insights, and structured experimentation to continuously improve integration performance and product impact.

Your Responsibilities

  • Integration & Marketplace Ownership: Own marketplace and integration vertical(s) such as CRMs, Forms, Email Systems, or Contact Platforms end-to-end, including roadmap, delivery, reliability, and adoption.
  • API & Configuration Definition: Review third-party API documentation and define integration configurations including authentication flows (OAuth, API keys), field mapping, data types, webhooks, polling strategies, pagination, rate limits, retries, and UI configuration controls.
  • Framework Gap Identification: Proactively identify missing workflow framework primitives and drive fixes before engineering execution begins to avoid reactive delivery slowdowns.
  • Adoption & Activation: Ensure integrations are deeply leveraged by improving trigger/action quality, importer flows, data parity, and workflow entry points such as hotlinks and breadcrumbs to increase activation and usage.
  • Reliability & Quality: Improve integration reliability, success rates, and execution consistency across triggers and actions.
  • Support Insight to Product Fixes: Analyze integration-related support tickets and ecosystem feedback to identify recurring friction and drive product improvements.
  • Tracking Metrics: Track integration time-to-ship, triggers/actions shipped per sprint, workflow usage tied to integrations, and reliability metrics to continuously improve performance.
  • Documentation & Enablement: Partner with documentation and AI teams to build high-quality help content and structured knowledge for both customers and AI-driven assistance.
  • Staying updated on industry trends in APIs, ecosystem integrations, automation platforms, and no-code/low-code tools to drive innovation within workflows.

Your Core Skills

  • Experience in one or more of the following:
  • Product Management
  • Platform or API-based Products
  • SaaS Integrations or Ecosystem Platforms
  • Passionate about building intuitive and scalable workflow automation solutions.
  • Ability to deeply understand user problems, spot friction in product flows, and make thoughtful trade-offs between power and simplicity.
  • Strong understanding of API concepts including OAuth, REST APIs, webhooks, pagination, rate limits, and data modeling.
  • Ability to deeply understand ecosystem workflows, anticipate framework gaps, and make thoughtful trade-offs between speed and scalability.
  • Comfort with digging into integration reliability metrics, workflow usage data, and support patterns to find actionable insights.
  • Understanding of product development lifecycle and experience working on SaaS platform products.
  • Strong analytical mindset to interpret activation funnels, adoption trends, and execution reliability.
  • Leadership and collaboration skills, with the ability to work cross-functionally and drive platform initiatives forward.
  • Understanding of automation tools and workflow best practices to enhance user experience.
  • Ability to deconstruct complex system interactions and simplify them into intuitive configuration experiences.
  • Familiarity with workflow automation platforms, CRMs, or integration ecosystems is a plus.
  • A systems thinker who thrives in a fast-paced, execution-driven environment.

Experience

  • 8+ years of Product Management experience ideally in SaaS or automation platforms
  • Proven track record of shipping complex, user-facing features in a fast-paced environment
  • Proven experience owning large-scale, customer-facing product platforms
  • Experience working on workflow automation tools or CRM platforms is a plus

Deep Understanding of:

  • Workflow builders, automations and no-code or low-code platforms
  • Product funnels: activation, retention, and reducing time to value
  • Identifying friction points in UX and translating feedback/support data into product decisions
  • Cross-team platform work: enabling other product teams through triggers, actions, and integrations
  • API integrations and ecosystem design principles
  • Workflow builders, automations and no-code or low-code platforms
  • Product funnels: activation, retention, and reducing time to value
  • Data parity, imports, synchronization systems, and reliability engineering
  • Cross-team platform work: enabling other product teams through triggers, actions, and shared primitives
  • Working with engineering on scalable frontend + backend architecture
  • Running feature discovery and validation loops for ecosystem products
  • Partnering closely with design to simplify complex integration setups
  • Writing clear, structured PRDs and communicating roadmap tradeoffs to stakeholders
  • Analyzing data from tools like Superset, Pendo or Tableau to inform product decisions

Why Should You Join Us?

At HighLevel, we foster an exciting and dynamic work environment driven by a passionate team. We believe in a collective responsibility where no task is considered someone else's job. Our unwavering focus is on providing value to our users, and we achieve this by delivering solutions swiftly through lean principles, allowing us to bring products to market in a matter of weeks rather than quarters. Every good idea is put to the test, ensuring that we maintain a high standard of innovation. We prioritise the well-being of our team, recognizing that by taking care of them, they can better serve our users. We embrace the concept of continuous and iterative improvement, understanding that progress is an ongoing journey. We are also a well funded & profitable company.

Join us at High Level, and you will have the opportunity to learn the intricacies of scaling a B2B SaaS startup and develop impactful products that cater to the needs of our customers.

Equal Employment Opportunity Information

The company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. As an employer subject to affirmative action regulations, we invite you to voluntarily provide the following demographic information. This information is used solely for compliance with government recordkeeping, reporting, and other legal requirements. Providing this information is voluntary and refusal to do so will not affect your application status. This data will be kept separate from your application and will not be used in the hiring decision.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Product Manager (ZTNA/Network Security domain)
description: About UsSophos is a cybersecurity leader defending 600,000 organizations globally with an AI-driven platform and expert-led services. Sophos meets organizations wherever they are in their security maturity and grows with them to defeat cyberattacks. Its solutions combine machine learning, automation, and real-time threat intelligence with frontline human expertise from Sophos X-Ops to deliver advanced, 24/7 threat monitoring, detection, and response.Sophos offers industry-leading managed detection and response (MDR) alongside a comprehensive portfolio of cybersecurity technologies — including endpoint, network, email, and cloud security, extended detection and response (XDR), identity threat detection and response (ITDR), and next-gen SIEM. Together with expert advisory services, these capabilities help organizations proactively reduce risk and respond faster, with the visibility and scalability needed to stay ahead of evolving threats.Sophos goes to market with a global partner ecosystem, including Managed Service Providers (MSPs), Managed Security Service Providers (MSSPs), resellers and distributors, marketplace integrations, and cyber risk partners, giving organizations the flexibility to choose trusted relationships when securing their business. Sophos is headquartered in Oxford, U.K. More information is available at www.sophos.com .Role SummaryThe Product Manager drives the strategy, discovery, and delivery of Sophos Workspace Protection, a Security Service Edge (SSE) solution. You will partner closely with engineering, design, sales, and customer success to build solutions that protect organizations as they embrace cloud-first and hybrid work environments. This Product Manager role owns the full product lifecycle, including identifying market opportunities and defining strategy through launch, adoption, and scaling. The primary focus of SSE capabilities will be Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA), the Sophos Protected Browser, and, to a lesser degree, the Sophos SD-RED devices. You'll operate at the intersection of security domain expertise, customer insight, and technical execution, with a global scope that includes regular collaboration with regional teams and periodic international travel. What makes this role unique is the combination of breadth and depth: you'll shape product direction in one of the fastest-evolving segments of cybersecurity, working across every function of the business.What You Will Do Own the end-to-end product lifecycle for capabilities within Workspace Protection, with a focus on ZTNA and Identity Provider integration, for private and SAAS app access use cases. This includes discovery and research through definition, delivery, launch, and iteration.  Define and maintain a clear product roadmap grounded in evolving security needs, competitive dynamics, and business objectives.  Develop expertise in SSE use cases, with particular focus on ZTNA and Protected Browser — understanding how businesses deploy these capabilities to secure remote access, reduce attack surfaces, and protect sensitive web-based workflows.  Conduct customer discovery through interviews, data analysis, and close collaboration with global and regional sales teams to uncover high-value security problems worth solving.  Translate complex security and networking requirements into clear product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria that enable engineering teams to deliver with confidence.  Partner with engineering to guide development, remove blockers, and ensure quality outcomes  Stay current on the competitive landscape to inform positioning and roadmap decisions.  Drive go-to-market planning in collaboration with sales, marketing, and customer success, including enablement on use cases and differentiators.  Define and track key product metrics, using data to inform decisions and communicate results to stakeholders.  Present product strategy, progress, and results to the product leadership team and Senior Management Team  Travel globally (~15% of the time, typically in 1-week trips) to engage with customers, regional teams, and partners  Report to the Director of Product Management What You Will Bring At least 5+ years of strong experience in product management having domain knowledge of cybersecurity, networking, or a related B2B SaaS environment  Strong understanding of the SSE market, including core capabilities such as ZTNA, SWG, CASB, DLP, and Protected Browser  Familiarity with Zero Trust architectures and how businesses adopt ZTNA to replace legacy VPN and secure access to private applications  Understanding of Protected Browser use cases — how businesses use browser-level controls to secure access to SaaS apps, protect sensitive data, and enable BYOD/contractor workflows  Proven track record of taking products from early discovery through launch and scaling in a technical domain  Ability to translate complex security and networking concepts into clear product strategy and actionable requirements  Comfort with data — you use quantitative and qualitative insights to drive prioritization and measure success  Excellent cross-functional communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority across engineering, sales, design, and leadership  Experience collaborating with global or distributed teams and engaging with enterprise customers and prospects  Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience; technical degree, or security certifications (CISSP, CCSP, etc.) are a plus #B2Ready to Join Us?At Sophos, we believe in the power of diverse perspectives to fuel innovation. Research shows that candidates sometimes hesitate to apply if they don't check every box in a job description. We challenge that notion. Your unique experiences and skills might be exactly what we need to enhance our team. Don't let a checklist hold you back – we encourage you to apply.What's Great About Sophos? Sophos operates a remote-first working model, making remote work the primary option for most employees. However, some roles may necessitate a hybrid approach. While we are a remote first organization, applicants must have legal authorization to work in the jurisdiction where the position is posted, without requiring employer sponsorship. Our people – we innovate and create, all of which are accompanied by a great sense of fun and team spirit Employee-led diversity and inclusion networks that build community and provide education and advocacy Annual charity and fundraising initiatives and volunteer days for employees to support local communities Global employee sustainability initiatives to reduce our environmental footprint Global fitness and trivia competitions to keep our bodies and minds sharp Global wellbeing days for employees to relax and recharge Monthly wellbeing webinars and training to support employee health and wellbeingOur Commitment To YouWe’re proud of the diverse and inclusive environment we have at Sophos, and we’re committed to ensuring equality of opportunity. We believe that diversity, combined with excellence, builds a better Sophos, so we encourage applicants who can contribute to the diversity of our team. All applicants will be treated in a fair and equal manner and in accordance with the law regardless of gender, sex, gender reassignment, marital status, race, religion or belief, color, age, military veteran status, disability, pregnancy, maternity or sexual orientation. We want to give you every opportunity to show us your best self, so if there are any adjustments we could make to the recruitment and selection process to support you, please let us know.Data ProtectionIf you choose to explore an opportunity, and subsequently share your CV or other personal details with Sophos, these details will be held by Sophos for 12 months in accordance with our Privacy Policy and used by our recruitment team to contact you regarding this or other relevant opportunities at Sophos. If you would like Sophos to delete or update your details at any time, please follow the steps set out in the Privacy Policy describing your individual rights. For more information on Sophos’ data protection practices, please consult our Privacy Policy Cybersecurity as a Service Delivered | SophosWe may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

The Product Manager drives the strategy, discovery, and delivery of Sophos Workspace Protection, a Security Service Edge (SSE) solution. You will partner closely with engineering, design, sales, and customer success to build solutions that protect organizations as they embrace cloud-first and hybrid work environments. This Product Manager role owns the full product lifecycle, including identifying market opportunities and defining strategy through launch, adoption, and scaling. The primary focus of SSE capabilities will be Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA), the Sophos Protected Browser, and, to a lesser degree, the Sophos SD-RED devices. You'll operate at the intersection of security domain expertise, customer insight, and technical execution, with a global scope that includes regular collaboration with regional teams and periodic international travel. What makes this role unique is the combination of breadth and depth: you'll shape product direction in one of the fastest-evolving segments of cybersecurity, working across every function of the business.

What You Will Do

  • Own the end-to-end product lifecycle for capabilities within Workspace Protection, with a focus on ZTNA and Identity Provider integration, for private and SAAS app access use cases. This includes discovery and research through definition, delivery, launch, and iteration.
  • Define and maintain a clear product roadmap grounded in evolving security needs, competitive dynamics, and business objectives.
  • Develop expertise in SSE use cases, with particular focus on ZTNA and Protected Browser — understanding how businesses deploy these capabilities to secure remote access, reduce attack surfaces, and protect sensitive web-based workflows.
  • Conduct customer discovery through interviews, data analysis, and close collaboration with global and regional sales teams to uncover high-value security problems worth solving.
  • Translate complex security and networking requirements into clear product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria that enable engineering teams to deliver with confidence.
  • Partner with engineering to guide development, remove blockers, and ensure quality outcomes
  • Stay current on the competitive landscape to inform positioning and roadmap decisions.
  • Drive go-to-market planning in collaboration with sales, marketing, and customer success, including enablement on use cases and differentiators.
  • Define and track key product metrics, using data to inform decisions and communicate results to stakeholders.
  • Present product strategy, progress, and results to the product leadership team and Senior Management Team
  • Travel globally (~15% of the time, typically in 1-week trips) to engage with customers, regional teams, and partners
  • Report to the Director of Product Management

What You Will Bring

  • At least 5+ years of strong experience in product management having domain knowledge of cybersecurity, networking, or a related B2B SaaS environment
  • Strong understanding of the SSE market, including core capabilities such as ZTNA, SWG, CASB, DLP, and Protected Browser
  • Familiarity with Zero Trust architectures and how businesses adopt ZTNA to replace legacy VPN and secure access to private applications
  • Understanding of Protected Browser use cases — how businesses use browser-level controls to secure access to SaaS apps, protect sensitive data, and enable BYOD/contractor workflows
  • Proven track record of taking products from early discovery through launch and scaling in a technical domain
  • Ability to translate complex security and networking concepts into clear product strategy and actionable requirements
  • Comfort with data — you use quantitative and qualitative insights to drive prioritization and measure success
  • Excellent cross-functional communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority across engineering, sales, design, and leadership
  • Experience collaborating with global or distributed teams and engaging with enterprise customers and prospects
  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience; technical degree, or security certifications (CISSP, CCSP, etc.) are a plus

#B2

Ready to Join Us?

At Sophos, we believe in the power of diverse perspectives to fuel innovation. Research shows that candidates sometimes hesitate to apply if they don't check every box in a job description. We challenge that notion. Your unique experiences and skills might be exactly what we need to enhance our team. Don't let a checklist hold you back – we encourage you to apply.

What's Great About Sophos?

  • Sophos operates a remote-first working model, making remote work the primary option for most employees. However, some roles may necessitate a hybrid approach. While we are a remote first organization, applicants must have legal authorization to work in the jurisdiction where the position is posted, without requiring employer sponsorship.
  • Our people – we innovate and create, all of which are accompanied by a great sense of fun and team spirit
  • Employee-led diversity and inclusion networks that build community and provide education and advocacy
  • Annual charity and fundraising initiatives and volunteer days for employees to support local communities
  • Global employee sustainability initiatives to reduce our environmental footprint
  • Global fitness and trivia competitions to keep our bodies and minds sharp
  • Global wellbeing days for employees to relax and recharge
  • Monthly wellbeing webinars and training to support employee health and wellbeing

Our Commitment To You

We’re proud of the diverse and inclusive environment we have at Sophos, and we’re committed to ensuring equality of opportunity. We believe that diversity, combined with excellence, builds a better Sophos, so we encourage applicants who can contribute to the diversity of our team. All applicants will be treated in a fair and equal manner and in accordance with the law regardless of gender, sex, gender reassignment, marital status, race, religion or belief, color, age, military veteran status, disability, pregnancy, maternity or sexual orientation. We want to give you every opportunity to show us your best self, so if there are any adjustments we could make to the recruitment and selection process to support you, please let us know.

Data Protection

If you choose to explore an opportunity, and subsequently share your CV or other personal details with Sophos, these details will be held by Sophos for 12 months in accordance with our Privacy Policy and used by our recruitment team to contact you regarding this or other relevant opportunities at Sophos. If you would like Sophos to delete or update your details at any time, please follow the steps set out in the Privacy Policy describing your individual rights. For more information on Sophos’ data protection practices, please consult our Privacy Policy Cybersecurity as a Service Delivered | Sophos

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

descriptionHtml:
About Us

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.

Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

Job Description

Want to build the future online checkout experience for Visa cardholders around the world?

Imagine being part of an agile team where your ideas transform the online checkout experience for millions of Visa cardholders globally. Visa Secure Remote Commerce (Visa SRC) Team is responsible for evaluating and executing on key strategic growth initiatives critical to the long-term success of Visa SRC. Our platform capabilities will be used by merchants and payment solution providers to enable SRC checkout experience in their Guest Checkout flows. Visa SRC is also offered by issuers as a feature of the card so that cardholders can shop at SRC enabled merchants as soon as the card is issued.

This role will also own the required analytics capabilities needed to track the success of this product at launch and over time, and articulate opportunities to improve through a metrics-based approach. In addition, the role will involve client interaction and assisting with preparing materials for both internal and external audiences.

We are seeking a Sr Product Manager who can help drive critical growth initiatives, both globally and regionally, in a complex, matrixed environment. The ideal candidate will be a detail-oriented, structured, analytical thinker with excellent communication skills and a strong interest in how products are developed and delivered. S/he enjoys variety and collaborating with people. S/he is thoughtful, curious and analytical with a desire to make an impact.

Responsibilities

  • Work cross-functionally with various teams across Visa to scope, document and implement a project plan to successfully deliver requirement features/enhancements of the product
  • Liaise with Risk, Technology, Design, Client Services, Internal & External Counsel, Corporate Strategy, Corporate Development, Risk, BU product managers and other applicable groups to ensure flawless product execution
  • Coordinate and manage initiative-specific marketing assets, i.e. explainer videos, decks, trade show materials
  • Provide status, action plan and recommendations to senior leadership on an ongoing basis
  • Drive the strategic roadmap, defining product and market requirements by understanding the needs of merchant ecosystem to scale SRC Acceptance globally
  • Manage and grow the required analytics capabilities to track the performance of the Visa Secure Remote Commerce, using tools and dashboards to build actionable insights
  • Managing, grooming, planning and execution of product backlog in a fast-paced agile environment
  • Establish detailed business requirements and specifications for existing and new services and products
  • Proactively identify gaps in the current product offering and lead the effort to develop solutions that drive business value
  • Focus on consumer feedback, market needs, client feedback, industry trends and competitive information
  • Participate in user experience iterations and help conduct user research to test product enhancements
  • Analyze product investment and performance to ensure consumer requirements are met and products are optimized for maximum return on investment and post-lunch performance

This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

Visa requires at least 3 days in office, expectations of these days will be confirmed by your Hiring Manager.

Qualifications

Basic Qualifications: 6 years of work experience with a Bachelor’s degree or an Advanced Degree (e.g., Masters, MBA, JD, MD, or PhD) Preferred Qualifications: 6 or more years of work experience or more than 4 years of work experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g., Masters, MBA, JD, MD) Strong analytical skills with familiarity of payment systems (card networks, ACH, RTP, digital wallets) Strong customer-facing communication skills and technical aptitude Individual works autonomously with ongoing supervision from manager and leadership Ability to explain complex business and technical concepts to broad audiences in an approachable way and strong documentation skills, including PowerPoint Detail oriented and highly execution focused by working in conjunction with various counterparts across multiple functional groups Ability to quickly learn about the internal Visa systems and how it all works together Product experience with B2B and B2B2C services that enable excellent user experiences, especially with services for e-commerce or payment systems or financial systems. Ability to influence, negotiate and drive results, ensuring that timelines are adhered to and any risks to the project are called out early Ability to derive insights from a combination of expertise in the product area, internal/external feedback & market research Embrace change and ambiguity in an ecosystem that is rapidly evolving. Excellent communication skills and executive presence. Advocate for the customer and demonstrate customer empathy. Articulate vision, strategy, and success (e.g., OKRs), makes prioritization decisions for releases and roadmap. 3+ year of global experience in the payments industry e.g. Payments processing and innovations in developed and emerging markets 2+ year experience with design thinking product development methodology

Visa is an EEO Employer

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

descriptionHtml:
Full-Time | Product & Technology | Remote (India Only) / Hybrid

About The Role

AI/ML Experience Required Telephony Knowledge a Plus

We are building a cutting-edge platform that tests Generative AI-based Interactive Voice Response (IVR) systems through real, automated phone calls — helping enterprises validate, benchmark, and improve their AI-powered voice experiences before they reach end customers. This is a high-impact space at the intersection of conversational AI, telephony, and quality engineering.

We are looking for a seasoned Senior Product Manager to own the end-to-end product lifecycle — from vision and strategy through discovery, delivery, and post-launch optimization. You will work cross-functionally with engineering, design, QA, sales, and marketing, and will play a pivotal role in shaping a product that sits at the forefront of AI-driven communication testing.

The ideal candidate has direct, hands-on experience managing AI/ML products and a strong intuition for how large language models and generative AI systems behave in production. Familiarity with telephony infrastructure and IVR systems will set you apart.

Key ResponsibilitiesStrategy & Vision

  • Define and own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for our Gen AI IVR testing platform, aligned with business goals.
  • Act as the voice of the customer while balancing long-term product scalability.
  • Monitor product performance using KPIs, analytics, and customer feedback to make data-driven decisions.

Discovery & Requirements

  • Gather, analyze, and prioritize requirements from clients, prospects, and internal stakeholders.
  • Translate customer needs and market insights around AI/ML and conversational voice systems into clear product requirements and user stories.
  • Lead product discovery, validation, and feature definition using data and customer feedback.
  • Prioritize the product backlog to balance customer value, business impact, and technical feasibility.

Cross-functional Collaboration

  • Collaborate closely with engineering, design, QA, sales, and marketing teams throughout the product lifecycle.
  • Partner with AI/ML engineers to define model evaluation criteria, testing frameworks, and performance benchmarks.
  • Conduct internal product demos and walkthroughs for stakeholders prior to customer release.
  • Manage stakeholder expectations across regions and time zones.
  • Drive successful product launches and coordinate go-to-market activities.

Documentation, Compliance & Launch Readiness

  • Own and maintain comprehensive product documentation, ensuring it is accurate, up to date, and accessible.
  • Ensure product compliance with regional regulations, security standards, and client requirements.
  • Ensure product readiness by aligning documentation, training, and release materials before launch.
  • Make data-driven decisions to improve product adoption, usability, and customer satisfaction.

What You BringRequired Qualifications

  • 10+ years of product management experience, with at least 3 years in a senior or lead PM role.
  • Proven track record of defining and shipping successful B2B or B2C products at scale.
  • Hands-on experience managing AI/ML products — you understand model lifecycles, evaluation pipelines, and the nuances of shipping AI-powered features to production. (Must Have)
  • Familiarity with Generative AI concepts including LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG, and AI evaluation/testing methodologies.
  • Strong ability to write clear, concise product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria.
  • Hands-on experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies and backlog management tools (e.g., Jira, Productboard, Linear, Redmine).
  • Excellent analytical skills — comfortable working with data, dashboards, and product analytics tools (e.g., Mixpanel, Amplitude, Looker).
  • Demonstrated experience managing cross-functional teams and executive stakeholders.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to present to diverse audiences.
  • Experience owning go-to-market coordination and product launch activities.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Knowledge of telephony systems, IVR platforms, and voice infrastructure (e.g., SIP, VoIP, Twilio, Genesys, Avaya, Amazon Connect) is a significant advantage. (Big Plus)
  • Experience with QA, testing, or DevOps tooling for AI systems.
  • Background in SaaS, conversational AI, contact centre, or enterprise voice platforms.
  • Experience with compliance-sensitive products (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, SOC 2, regional data regulations).
  • Experience working in globally distributed teams across multiple time zones.
  • Familiarity with product-led growth (PLG) strategies.
  • MBA or equivalent advanced degree is a plus, but not required.

Skills: product planning,jira,ml,telephony,product launch,compliance,gen ai,saas,backlog management,agile,b2b,documentation,product management,ai,ivr

title: Sr. Product Manager - Recommendations, Boosts & Ads Monetization
description: About UsHighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. With 250 terabytes of distributed data, 250+ microservices and over 1 million domain names supported, our architecture is built for performance, resilience and long-term scalability.Our peopleWith over 2,000 team members across 10+ countries, HighLevel operates as a global, remote-first organization built for speed and ownership. We value initiative, clarity and execution, creating space for ambitious people to build systems that support millions of businesses worldwide. Here, innovation thrives, ideas are celebrated and people come first, no matter where they call home.Our impactEvery month, HighLevel enables more than 1.5 billion messages, 200 million leads and 20 million conversations for the more than 1 million businesses we support. Behind those numbers are real people building independence, expanding opportunity and creating measurable impact. We’re proud to be a part of that.Learn more about us on our YouTube Channel or Blog PostsRole OverviewWe’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to lead the strategy and execution for our recommendation systems, Boosts (cross-promotion), and Ads monetization initiatives.You’ll own the end-to-end product vision for how creators, publishers, and businesses grow and monetize their audience across our platform – inspired by ecosystems like Substack, Beehiiv, and their recommendation/ads networks – and turn that into a coherent roadmap and shipped outcomes. This is a highly strategic role with massive impact on revenue, creator growth, and network effects.What You’ll OwnStrategy & Vision:  Own the north-star vision and roadmap for: Subscriber/content recommendations surfaces Boosts / cross-promotion marketplace Ads monetization (on-network and partner inventory) Translate company strategy into clear, measurable product outcomes (e.g. revenue per subscriber, LTV, ARPU, activation & retention). Map and deeply understand the ecosystem: creators, advertisers, agencies, and end readers/customers.Recommendations – New Format & Growth Engine:  Define and evolve a recommendation system that helps users discover the most relevant content, creators, and offers. Partner with Data/ML to Design ranking & recommendation algorithms Define training data needs and feedback loops Ship experiments that improve click-through, conversion, and downstream value. Own the UX of recommendation surfaces (widgets, feeds, carousels, in-product placements) to balance growth, user value, and trust. Build experimentation and measurement frameworks for A/B tests and multi-variant experiments around recommendations.Monetize (Boosts) Cross-Promotion MarketplaceOwn the Boosts product: a marketplace where creators/brands can promote each other, grow lists, and drive incremental subscribers/customers. Define matching logic, pricing/credits models, eligibility rules, and quality/reputation signals. Design simple but powerful flows for: Buying and managing boosts, Approving/reviewing partners, Monitoring performance (CPC, CPA, net new subs, ROI). Ensure the marketplace has the right guardrails to prevent abuse, spam, and low-quality promotion.Execution & Cross-Functional Leadership Translate vision and PRDs (Recommendations – New Format, Monitize Boosts, Monitize Ads, etc.) into prioritized, outcome-driven roadmaps. Run structured discovery: interviews, prototypes, data deep-dives, and competitive analysis (Substack, Beehiiv, etc.). Collaborate closely with: Engineering to plan, scope, and deliver high-quality releases Design to create intuitive, scalable UX Data to instrument metrics and build robust dashboards Sales/CS/Marketing to shape GTM, positioning, and feedback loops Drive alignment and narrative: clearly communicate tradeoffs, decisions, and progress to stakeholders and leadership.What You’ll Bring 6+ years in Product Management Experience in one or more of: Recommendations / personalization / ML-driven products Ads platforms or ad networks Marketplaces or cross-promotion/growth products Creator platforms, email/newsletters, or content networks (Substack, Beehiiv, etc.). Strong analytical and technical fluency: Comfortable working with Data/ML teams, Can define robust metrics and experimentation plans, Can reason about system design, APIs, and data flows at a high level. Proven track record of moving key business metrics (revenue, ARPU, activation, retention, engagement). Demonstrated ability to lead without authority, influence cross-functional partners, and drive alignment across product, engineering, and GTM. Excellent written and verbal communication; can create crisp narratives for both executive stakeholders and individual contributors.How Success Will Be Measured (First 12 Months) A clear, aligned 12–18 month roadmap for recommendations, Boosts, and Ads is defined and in execution. Meaningful improvements in: Recommendation performance (CTR, conversion, downstream LTV) Boosts marketplace health (volume, match quality, ROI for participants) Ads monetization metrics (revenue, RPM, fill, advertiser retention) Strong internal adoption and understanding of these products by Sales, CS, and Marketing. You are seen as the single-threaded owner and strategic leader for this domain.Equal Employment Opportunity InformationThe company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. As an employer subject to affirmative action regulations, we invite you to voluntarily provide the following demographic information. This information is used solely for compliance with government recordkeeping, reporting, and other legal requirements. Providing this information is voluntary and refusal to do so will not affect your application status. This data will be kept separate from your application and will not be used in the hiring decision.We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
descriptionHtml:
About Us

HighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.

To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.

Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. With 250 terabytes of distributed data, 250+ microservices and over 1 million domain names supported, our architecture is built for performance, resilience and long-term scalability.

Our people

With over 2,000 team members across 10+ countries, HighLevel operates as a global, remote-first organization built for speed and ownership. We value initiative, clarity and execution, creating space for ambitious people to build systems that support millions of businesses worldwide. Here, innovation thrives, ideas are celebrated and people come first, no matter where they call home.

Our impact

Every month, HighLevel enables more than 1.5 billion messages, 200 million leads and 20 million conversations for the more than 1 million businesses we support. Behind those numbers are real people building independence, expanding opportunity and creating measurable impact. We’re proud to be a part of that.

Learn more about us on our YouTube Channel or Blog Posts

Role Overview

We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to lead the strategy and execution for our recommendation systems, Boosts (cross-promotion), and Ads monetization initiatives.

You’ll own the end-to-end product vision for how creators, publishers, and businesses grow and monetize their audience across our platform – inspired by ecosystems like Substack, Beehiiv, and their recommendation/ads networks – and turn that into a coherent roadmap and shipped outcomes. This is a highly strategic role with massive impact on revenue, creator growth, and network effects.

What You’ll Own

Strategy & Vision:

  • Own the north-star vision and roadmap for:
  • Subscriber/content recommendations surfaces
  • Boosts / cross-promotion marketplace
  • Ads monetization (on-network and partner inventory)
  • Translate company strategy into clear, measurable product outcomes (e.g. revenue per subscriber, LTV, ARPU, activation & retention).
  • Map and deeply understand the ecosystem: creators, advertisers, agencies, and end readers/customers.

Recommendations – New Format & Growth Engine:

  • Define and evolve a recommendation system that helps users discover the most relevant content, creators, and offers.
  • Partner with Data/ML to
  • Design ranking & recommendation algorithms
  • Define training data needs and feedback loops
  • Ship experiments that improve click-through, conversion, and downstream value.
  • Own the UX of recommendation surfaces (widgets, feeds, carousels, in-product placements) to balance growth, user value, and trust.
  • Build experimentation and measurement frameworks for A/B tests and multi-variant experiments around recommendations.

Monetize (Boosts)

  • Cross-Promotion MarketplaceOwn the Boosts product: a marketplace where creators/brands can promote each other, grow lists, and drive incremental subscribers/customers.
  • Define matching logic, pricing/credits models, eligibility rules, and quality/reputation signals.
  • Design simple but powerful flows for: Buying and managing boosts, Approving/reviewing partners, Monitoring performance (CPC, CPA, net new subs, ROI).
  • Ensure the marketplace has the right guardrails to prevent abuse, spam, and low-quality promotion.

Execution & Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Translate vision and PRDs (Recommendations – New Format, Monitize Boosts, Monitize Ads, etc.) into prioritized, outcome-driven roadmaps.
  • Run structured discovery: interviews, prototypes, data deep-dives, and competitive analysis (Substack, Beehiiv, etc.).
  • Collaborate closely with:
  • Engineering to plan, scope, and deliver high-quality releases
  • Design to create intuitive, scalable UX
  • Data to instrument metrics and build robust dashboards
  • Sales/CS/Marketing to shape GTM, positioning, and feedback loops
  • Drive alignment and narrative: clearly communicate tradeoffs, decisions, and progress to stakeholders and leadership.

What You’ll Bring

  • 6+ years in Product Management
  • Experience in one or more of:
  • Recommendations / personalization / ML-driven products
  • Ads platforms or ad networks
  • Marketplaces or cross-promotion/growth products
  • Creator platforms, email/newsletters, or content networks (Substack, Beehiiv, etc.).
  • Strong analytical and technical fluency: Comfortable working with Data/ML teams, Can define robust metrics and experimentation plans, Can reason about system design, APIs, and data flows at a high level.
  • Proven track record of moving key business metrics (revenue, ARPU, activation, retention, engagement).
  • Demonstrated ability to lead without authority, influence cross-functional partners, and drive alignment across product, engineering, and GTM.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication; can create crisp narratives for both executive stakeholders and individual contributors.

How Success Will Be Measured (First 12 Months)

  • A clear, aligned 12–18 month roadmap for recommendations, Boosts, and Ads is defined and in execution.
  • Meaningful improvements in:
  • Recommendation performance (CTR, conversion, downstream LTV)
  • Boosts marketplace health (volume, match quality, ROI for participants)
  • Ads monetization metrics (revenue, RPM, fill, advertiser retention)
  • Strong internal adoption and understanding of these products by Sales, CS, and Marketing.
  • You are seen as the single-threaded owner and strategic leader for this domain.

Equal Employment Opportunity Information

The company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. As an employer subject to affirmative action regulations, we invite you to voluntarily provide the following demographic information. This information is used solely for compliance with government recordkeeping, reporting, and other legal requirements. Providing this information is voluntary and refusal to do so will not affect your application status. This data will be kept separate from your application and will not be used in the hiring decision.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

descriptionHtml:
About Us

HighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.

To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.

Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. With 250 terabytes of distributed data, 250+ microservices and over 1 million domain names supported, our architecture is built for performance, resilience and long-term scalability.

Our people

With over 2,000 team members across 10+ countries, HighLevel operates as a global, remote-first organization built for speed and ownership. We value initiative, clarity and execution, creating space for ambitious people to build systems that support millions of businesses worldwide. Here, innovation thrives, ideas are celebrated and people come first, no matter where they call home.

Our impact

Every month, HighLevel enables more than 1.5 billion messages, 200 million leads and 20 million conversations for the more than 1 million businesses we support. Behind those numbers are real people building independence, expanding opportunity and creating measurable impact. We’re proud to be a part of that.

Learn more about us on our YouTube Channel or Blog Posts

Role Overview

We are hiring a Senior Product Manager – AI Growth to drive adoption, engagement, and monetization across HighLevel’s AI product suite. This role is designed for builder PMs who combine strong product instincts with hands-on execution. You will operate as a “Team of One” product owner responsible for identifying growth opportunities, shipping experiments, and building AI-powered experiences that increase product usage and revenue.

What You’ll Do

AI Growth Strategy

  • Identify where AI can most meaningfully improve activation, retention, expansion, and

revenue.

  • Translate AI capabilities into clear user value and growth loops.
  • Define the roadmap for AI-driven adoption across HighLevel products.

Activation & Adoption

  • Own the AI usage funnel: discovery → activation → depth → retention → expansion.
  • Design onboarding, prompts, and product experiences that make AI features easy and valuable

to use.

  • Improve AI discoverability and engagement across the product.

Experimentation & Growth Engineering

  • Run rapid growth experiments across UX, pricing, feature access, and messaging.
  • Prototype experiments using AI development tools and lightweight coding frameworks.
  • Partner with engineering to scale successful experiments.

Monetization & Pricing

  • Identify opportunities to monetize AI capabilities through pricing, packaging, and usage-based

models.

  • Partner with GTM teams to launch and iterate on AI offerings.

Analytics & Insights

  • Define metrics including activation, WAU/MAU, feature depth, and expansion revenue.
  • Instrument events and analyze funnels to guide iteration.

What Success Looks Like

  • Increased adoption of AI features
  • Higher engagement with AI workflows
  • Revenue growth driven by AI products
  • Faster experimentation cycles

Minimum Qualifications

  • 8–10 years of product management experience
  • Experience working on growth, adoption, or monetization problems
  • Experience shipping AI-powered features or products
  • Strong data-driven decision making

Preferred Qualifications

  • Hands-on coding or prototyping experience (1–2+ years)
  • Experience with LLM APIs, AI tools, or agent frameworks
  • Familiarity with SaaS growth loops and product-led growth
  • Portfolio, GitHub, or prototypes demonstrating builder capability

Who This Role Is For

  • Product managers who build and ship.
  • Builders who prototype ideas and move quickly from concept to product.
  • PMs who leverage AI tools, automation, and code to accelerate product development.

Why Should You Join Us?

At HighLevel, we foster an exciting and dynamic work environment driven by a passionate team. We believe in a collective responsibility where no task is considered someone else's job. Our unwavering focus is on providing value to our users, and we achieve this by delivering solutions swiftly through lean principles, allowing us to bring products to market in a matter of weeks rather than quarters. Every good idea is put to the test, ensuring that we maintain a high standard of innovation. We prioritise the well-being of our team, recognizing that by taking care of them, they can better serve our users. We embrace the concept of continuous and iterative improvement, understanding that progress is an ongoing journey. We are also a well funded & profitable company.

Join us at High Level, and you will have the opportunity to learn the intricacies of scaling a B2B SaaS startup and develop impactful products that cater to the needs of our customers.

Equal Employment Opportunity Information

The company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. As an employer subject to affirmative action regulations, we invite you to voluntarily provide the following demographic information. This information is used solely for compliance with government recordkeeping, reporting, and other legal requirements. Providing this information is voluntary and refusal to do so will not affect your application status. This data will be kept separate from your application and will not be used in the hiring decision.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

descriptionHtml:
Why Choose Bottomline?

Are you ready to transform the way businesses pay and get paid? Bottomline is a global leader in business payments and cash management, with over 35 years of experience and moving more than $16 trillion in payments annually. We're looking for passionate individuals to join our team and help drive impactful results for our customers. If you're dedicated to delighting customers and promoting growth and innovation - we want you on our team!

Product Manager – AI & Data Science

Location: Bangalore

Experience: 6-8 years

Role Summary

We are seeking a Product Manager with strong AI & Data Science product experience to drive AI-led innovation across our product portfolio. This is a strategic AI product management role, focused on AI vision, governance, research, and prioritization—not day-to-day delivery or sprint execution.

This PM will work horizontally across product lines, partnering with Product Line PMs and AI/Data Science/CTO engineering teams to ensure we invest in the right AI problems, apply responsible AI principles, and deliver high-impact, scalable AI features.

Key Responsibilities

AI Product Strategy & Roadmap

  • Define and own the AI/Data Science product vision aligned to business outcomes.
  • Identify and prioritize high-impact AI use cases across business payments network, corporate payment solutions and banking solutions
  • Shape a multi‑quarter AI roadmap focused on measurable customer and business impact.

AI Governance & Responsible AI

  • Ensure AI initiatives comply with responsible AI principles (privacy, security, explainability, regulatory expectations).
  • Establish and run AI intake, prioritization, and review mechanisms with Product Line PMs and engineering.
  • Act as the central owner for AI product governance across teams.

Research & Solutioning

  • Lead problem discovery and solution research—customer pain points, data readiness, feasibility, and ROI.
  • Partner with AI/Data Science engineers to evaluate model approaches, trade-offs, and constraints.

Cross‑functional Leadership (No Sprint Ownership)

  • Collaborate with Product Line PMs who own execution and delivery.
  • Work closely with AI/Data Science, Data Platforms, Architecture and Legal/Compliance
  • Influence priorities through clarity, insight, and impact—without direct authority.

Impact & Measurement

  • Define success metrics for AI features (adoption, efficiency, revenue, risk reduction etc.).
  • Ensure AI investments deliver real customer and business outcomes, not just technical outputs.

Required Qualifications

  • 6–8 years of product management experience with hands-on AI/ML product exposure.
  • Proven experience delivering AI-powered enterprise or B2B SaaS products.
  • Strong understanding of applied ML concepts, data pipelines, and AI lifecycle.
  • Experience working in regulated or complex domains (FinTech, Payments, Banking preferred).
  • Strong stakeholder management, structured thinking, and written communication skills.
  • Experience operating effectively in matrixed, global organizations.

We welcome talent at all career stages and are dedicated to understanding and supporting additional needs. We're proud to be an equal opportunity employer, committed to creating an inclusive and open environment for everyone.

descriptionHtml:
About The Company

Founders today are building global companies from day one — but the systems that manage their money were built for a different era. Aspire exists to change that!

We’re building the financial operating system for global founders, bringing banking, software, and automation into a single platform so businesses can move faster across borders and stay focused on building.

Aspire is built by people who think from first principles, care deeply about solving hard problems, and take real ownership of their work. Our team brings global experience from leading fintech and technology companies, and many of us are former founders and operators who understand what it takes to build thoughtfully, make trade-offs, and deliver at scale in a global environment.

Backed by leading global investors including Y Combinator, Peak XV, and Lightspeed, Aspire has been trusted by more than 50,000 startups and growing businesses worldwide to manage their finances since 2018. Together with partners like J.P. Morgan, Visa, and Wise, we’re building for the next generation of global companies.

Senior Product Manager - API Infrastructure (Payments)

About The Team

At Aspire, we believe that building exceptional products starts with deeply understanding our customers. Our Product Team is the voice of the customer within the company — relentlessly curious, data-informed, and impact-driven. We lead with research, insights, and empathy to define the right problems to solve, and work cross-functionally to craft solutions that create real value for our users and their businesses. At the same time, we stay laser-focused on maximizing impact for Aspire, ensuring our products contribute meaningfully to growth, scalability, and long-term success. As a fintech, we also design with trust at the core — embedding risk management, security, and regulatory compliance into every stage of the product lifecycle.

About The Role

  • We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to own our Payments API platform and underlying API infrastructure, which powers all payment experiences across our enterprise and institutional clients. This role is responsible for defining, scaling, and operating a reliable, developer-friendly, and compliant payments platform that enables fast product iteration across markets and use cases.
  • You will work closely with Engineering, Compliance, Risk, and Operations to abstract complex payment rails and regulatory requirements into clean, reusable APIs.
  • Own the Payments API roadmap: Define and drive the roadmap for core payments APIs and shared infrastructure supporting pay-ins, payouts, transfers, and transaction lifecycle management.
  • End-to-end API ownership: Design APIs across initiation, processing, settlement, reconciliation, errors, retries, and reversals with consistency and predictability.
  • Developer experience (DX): Partner with Engineering and Design to deliver intuitive APIs, clear documentation, SDKs, webhooks, and error handling standards.
  • Translate complexity into platforms: Convert banking, partner, and regulatory constraints into scalable platform primitives and workflows.
  • Platform metrics & reliability: Define and track KPIs such as API uptime, latency, error rates, payment success rates, and SLA adherence.
  • Compliance & security by design: Ensure APIs meet regulatory, audit, and security requirements across markets while remaining easy to integrate.
  • Continuous improvement: Identify integration friction, drive platform enhancements, and lead API versioning and deprecations with minimal disruption.
  • Product discovery: Validate new API capabilities with internal teams and key customers before launch.

Minimum Qualifications

  • 6–8 years of experience in product management or platform roles within payments, FinTech, or financial services.
  • Strong understanding of payments infrastructure and APIs, including transaction orchestration, settlement models, and failure handling.
  • Proven experience building or scaling API-first or developer platforms.
  • Experience working cross-functionally with Engineering, Compliance, Risk, and Operations in regulated environments.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and product specification skills.
  • Solid understanding of AI concepts and experience applying AI tools to monitoring, anomaly detection, risk signals, or operational automation.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Deep payments systems expertise: Strong understanding of payment rails and schemes across markets, including real-time payments, batch clearing, card networks, and international transfers, with hands-on experience handling settlement, reconciliation, and failure modes.
  • Technically fluent platform PM: Proven experience working on API-first, distributed systems, including RESTful API design, webhooks, idempotency, async processing, and event-driven architectures.
  • Infrastructure-scale mindset: Experience partnering with engineering teams on high-availability, high-throughput financial platforms, with a strong grasp of reliability, resiliency, observability, and incident management.
  • Compliance-aware by design: Practical experience embedding regulatory, audit, and risk controls directly into platform and API design, rather than treating them as downstream concerns.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Aspire. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Aspire may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Aspire's Candidate Privacy Notice

We prioritise the well-being of our team, recognizing that by taking care of them, they can better serve our users. We embrace the concept of continuous and iterative improvement, understanding that progress is an ongoing journey. We are also a well funded & profitable company.Join us at High Level, and you will have the opportunity to learn the intricacies of scaling a B2B SaaS startup and develop impactful products that cater to the needs of our customers.Equal Employment Opportunity InformationThe company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. As an employer subject to affirmative action regulations, we invite you to voluntarily provide the following demographic information. This information is used solely for compliance with government recordkeeping, reporting, and other legal requirements. Providing this information is voluntary and refusal to do so will not affect your application status. This data will be kept separate from your application and will not be used in the hiring decision.We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans.
